Out-of-sample forward testing confirms edge for 50+ trades
exit_conditions: Live trading expectancy drops below backtest expectancy by >20%
Win rate falls below 40% of historical baseline

rule_text: A trader should trade only when statistical edge is present (favorable expectancy), and avoid market environments where price action is random, sizing positions according to win rate and reward/risk ratio.

Market exhibits directional structure (not random walk)
Statistical confidence in edge > 95%
exit_conditions: Exit when edge signal disappears
Exit when regime shifts to randomness

rule_text: A trader should pay heightened attention to price levels where significant emotional events (fear, hope, pain) occurred in the past, because market memory causes nonrandom price behavior when those levels are revisited.
entry_conditions: Price revisits a historical extreme from past 252+ trading days
Retest confirmed by volume >= 150% of 20-day MA
Price holds within 0.5% of extreme level for 2+ bars
Emotional reversal pattern present (e.g., pin bar, engulfing)
exit_conditions: Close beyond opposite extreme of retest
Volume dries up for 3 bars
Time stop: 10 bars from entry

rule_text: Detect and trade the predictable price tendencies created by the clustered, pulsed execution pattern of large-scale buyers and the market's memory of previous inflection points.
entry_conditions: Aggressive buying spurt detected (volume spike >150% of MA volume)
Market absorption pause observed (volume drop to <60% of spike level)
Second buying spurt detected (volume spike >150% again)
Price holds above first spurt's high by minimum 0.5% (memory of inflection)
Confirmation candle closes above resistance created by previous inflection
exit_conditions: Break below the first spurt's high by 0.5%
Absorption phase extends beyond 2x average pause duration

rule_text: Buying and selling pressure creates non-random price patterns in market structure; a trader should identify and exploit these pressure-driven patterns by detecting where urgency in order flow (large bids/asks) manifests as directional price action.
entry_conditions: Large order imbalance detected (bid/ask ratio > 2.0 standard deviations from mean)
Price movement persists in pressure direction for minimum 3 confirmed bars
Volume supports the direction (volume > 20-period average)
Entry only on pullback into support/resistance after initial pressure move
exit_conditions: Price closes below/above relevant support/resistance by 1 ATR
Order pressure reverses (bid/ask ratio reverts to mean)
Time-based: exit if no follow-through after 5 bars

rule_text: A trader should initiate trades only at points where measurable buying or selling pressure imbalance exists, rather than trading patterns themselves.
entry_conditions: Volume-weighted price movement in one direction exceeds opposing direction by 65% or more over last N bars
Bid-ask spread imbalance (one side absorbs 3+ times the other side's volume)
Price closes beyond 2-bar range with volume confirmation
exit_conditions: Imbalance reverses or falls below 55% threshold
Bid-ask ratio equalizes to 1.5:1 or less
Fixed stop-loss at 2% account risk

rule_text: A trader should use multiple timeframes—a higher timeframe for context and a lower timeframe for entry precision—to improve risk management and increase trading plan expectancy.
entry_conditions: HTF shows established trend direction (price above/below key moving average or swing structure)
TTF shows aligned structure in same direction (higher highs/lows or support/resistance confirmation)
LTF shows precise entry signal (pullback to moving average, minor support, or pattern completion)
exit_conditions: HTF structural support/resistance violated
TTF closes below entry bar range
Predefined stop loss at 2x TTF average true range

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch. 1 (P1:OTA), pp. 17–46
rule_text: A trader should use multiple timeframes—a higher timeframe for context and a lower timeframe for entry precision—to improve risk management and increase trading plan expectancy.

LTF shows entry signal aligned with HTF direction (breakout, pullback, or pattern in HTF direction)
exit_conditions: HTF swing point or key level violated

Predefined stop loss at 1.5x LTF average true range

rule_text: A trader should use multiple timeframes—a higher timeframe for context and a lower timeframe for entry precision—to improve risk management and increase trading plan expectancy.

LTF shows technically sound entry signal (breakout, pattern, or momentum signal) regardless of HTF structure alignment
exit_conditions: LTF closes beyond 2x entry bar range or violates entry pattern

HTF closes beyond key structure level

rule_text: A trader should select multiple time frames with proportional relationships (factor of 3-5) to observe price action at different resolutions without redundant information.
entry_conditions: Primary timeframe selected (e.g., 30-minute chart)
Secondary timeframe = Primary / 5 (e.g., 6-minute chart)
Tertiary timeframe = Primary / 25 (e.g., 1.2-minute/tick-based chart)
Confirm directional alignment across all three timeframes before entry
exit_conditions: Exit when middle timeframe shows reversal
Exit when alignment breaks across timeframes

exit_conditions: Price bounces back >60% of move within 3 bars
Two-way trading resumes (back-and-forth action)
Order flow reverses or dries up

rule_text: A trader should identify whether a large price impulse will reverse to prior levels or develop into a trending feedback loop, and position accordingly based on early confirmation of continued directional movement.

Confirm with volume increase in direction of continuation
exit_conditions: Price retraces to 50% of impulse magnitude

Time stop: exit if no new impulse within 5 bars

rule_text: A trader should identify and use pivot point hierarchies (especially third-order pivots) as structural context to delineate market inflection points and inform directional bias, while explicitly planning rules for edge cases where multiple pivots of the same order occur consecutively.
entry_conditions: Third-order pivot (3+ nested pivot orders) identified on daily or higher timeframe
Price approaches third-order pivot level with volume confirmation
No more than one second-order pivot in the preceding 5 bars
Entry only after price closes beyond the third-order pivot level
exit_conditions: Exit if next third-order pivot level is violated in adverse direction
Exit if invalidating structure forms (more than 2 consecutive same-order pivots)

rule_text: A trader should monitor for breaks in established trend patterns (failure of higher pivot lows/highs in uptrends) as early warning signals of potential trend changes, but should not trade on this signal alone without confirmation.

rule_text: A trader should monitor for breaks in established trend patterns (failure of higher pivot lows/highs in uptrends) as early warning signals of potential trend changes, but should not trade on this signal alone without confirmation.
entry_conditions: Uptrend established: series of at least 2 higher pivot lows and higher pivot highs

Confirmation: Price closes beyond broken pivot level on 1 bar
exit_conditions: Exit on first sign of reversal back into previous pattern
Time-based stop: exit if no directional follow-through within 3-5 bars

rule_text: A trader should infer the lower timeframe price action that created a higher timeframe bar in order to understand buying and selling imbalance and improve chart reading accuracy.
entry_conditions: Observe a significant bar on the primary timeframe (e.g., 4H or 1D)
Examine the immediate lower timeframe (e.g., 1H if primary is 4H)

Confirm buying or selling pressure aligns with the higher timeframe bar direction
exit_conditions: Lower timeframe structure reverses direction
Imbalance closes by 50%+

rule_text: A trader should examine multiple lower timeframe scenarios that could produce observed price action rather than locking into a single interpretation, to avoid blindness to what is actually occurring in the market.

Enter only when lower timeframe price action confirms the most likely scenario with opposing confirmation bar
exit_conditions: Exit if lower timeframe price action contradicts assumed formation scenario
Exit on close below/above key level established in lower timeframe analysis

rule_text: A trader should identify loss of momentum in a trend by observing reluctant highs, declining bar sizes, absence of large trend bars, and multiple failed breakouts above prior extremes across multiple timeframes.
entry_conditions: Higher highs made with declining magnitude (each new high exceeds prior by less than previous increment)
Average bar close-to-close range contracts below 30-day moving average of range
Zero large trend bars (bars that open in bottom 20% and close in top 20% of range) in last 8 bars
Three or more failed tests above the highest high of last 5 bars
Lower timeframe confirms all four signals simultaneously
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at first target: 1.5× ATR below entry
Exit 50% on retest of recent high with rejection
Hard stop: 2× ATR above last high

rule_text: A trader should identify reversals and conviction changes by analyzing lower timeframe price action (multiple tests and failures at resistance/support) that may appear as simple consolidation on higher timeframes, because lower resolution reveals the true shift in buyer/seller conviction.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe (e.g., 15-min) shows 3+ tests above recent high with pullbacks below that high
Each test fails to hold above the high
Higher timeframe (e.g., 4-hour) shows gentle rounding or consolidation pattern
Volume declining on failed tests indicates weakening conviction
exit_conditions: Stop loss: above the highest failed test + 1 ATR
Take profit: 1.5x risk

rule_text: A trader should recognize and trade complex pullbacks consisting of a three-legged structure (initial pullback, failed trend resumption, countertrend move) as they are common patterns in extended trends and represent higher-probability reversal or continuation setups.
entry_conditions: Price is in an extended trend (confirmed on higher timeframe)
Initial pullback leg completes and is measurable
Failed trend resumption attempt occurs (second leg)
Third leg (countertrend) develops and reaches 90-100% of first leg length
Pattern observed on 2-minute or 5-minute lower timeframe while trend visible on higher timeframe
exit_conditions: Exit if price fails to continue in trend direction after third leg
Exit if pattern breaks below first leg low (invalidation)

Third leg develops and reaches 75-95% of first leg length
Pattern identified on 5-minute or 15-minute lower timeframe

Exit if structure invalidated by lower-low/lower-high pattern

rule_text: A trader should recognize and trade complex pullbacks consisting of a three-legged structure (initial pullback, failed trend resumption, countertrend move) as they are common patterns in extended trends and represent higher-probability reversal or continuation setups.

Pattern recognized on current lower timeframe (2-min, 5-min, or 15-min)
exit_conditions: Exit on breakeven + 1 ATR if pattern fails to complete
Exit on reversal candle or close below entry logic level

rule_text: Deliberate manual chart analysis practice develops pattern recognition ability and market perception, and traders should commit to consistent hand-drawn charting for a minimum duration to internalize market structure before evaluating trading performance.

rule_text: A trader should identify and enter positions during the accumulation phase (sideways range with large player activity and low public awareness) before the markup phase begins, as this phase precedes sustained uptrends driven by public participation.
entry_conditions: Price consolidated in sideways range for minimum 20 bars
Range-bound price action with low volatility (ATR < 0.5 * 20-bar average ATR)
Volume profile shows institutional accumulation (large trades without price movement)
Price breaks above upper accumulation level with volume surge (>150% of 20-bar average volume)
exit_conditions: Close below accumulation range
Distribution phase detected (price strength with declining volume)

rule_text: A trader should initiate short positions during the markdown phase that follows a distribution phase, because panic selling by the public marks the end of the downtrend and creates a reversal opportunity.
entry_conditions: Price has completed a distribution phase (identified by reduced volume on up moves, increased volume on down moves)
Price breaks below the distribution range support level
Volume spike > 1.5x average occurs on the breakdown
RSI(14) drops below 40, indicating weakness
Price closes below 20-period moving average
exit_conditions: Cover 50% of short at first sign of panic selling (volume > 3x average)
Cover remaining 50% when price stabilizes above markdown low
Hard stop if price closes back above distribution range

rule_text: Large accumulation players test the bottom of a range with small probing sells to discover the lowest price at which consistent buying demand exists, before committing to larger purchases.

No follow-through selling below the test low on next 2 bars
exit_conditions: Price closes below the test low on high volume
Range breaks to downside with conviction
20-bar stop below the test low

rule_text: A trader should resist the psychological urge to fade (short) early trend moves that appear to violate long-established price ranges, as this contrarian impulse typically leads to losses when trends are initiating.
entry_conditions: Price has broken above historical range by at least 1 standard deviation
Price has closed above breakout level for at least 3 consecutive periods
Volume on breakout bars is above 50th percentile of recent volume
Emotional urge to short is present (contrarian indicator to ignore)
exit_conditions: If forced into counter-trend trade: exit immediately on first profitable tick
Better: do not enter counter-trend trade; enter trend-following trade instead

Price drops recorded with below-median volume relative to recent range
No follow-through buying on drops over past 20 bars
exit_conditions: Price breaks above upper range bound on heavy volume
Classic accumulation signal emerges (large support wick, volume spike up)

rule_text: Recognize when a market in distribution is being misinterpreted by the public as a continuation opportunity, and avoid buying into false breakout signals that the crowd mistakenly views as proof of a new uptrend.
entry_conditions: Market has advanced 30%+ in recent trend
Price has failed to break above prior resistance 2 times within 8 bars
Volume on breakout attempt is below 1.0x average trend volume
Public sentiment remains constructive despite failed breakout
exit_conditions: Price closes above resistance with volume spike >2.0x average
New trend leg initiates with momentum

rule_text: A trader should be cautious of or fade consensus bullish sentiment that emerges after a protracted distribution phase, as real downtrends often begin from environments of optimism and complacency where public agreement on higher prices is universal.
entry_conditions: Price consolidates in defined range (distribution) for 20+ bars

Volume decreases during distribution phase (below 50th percentile)
Price breaks below the lower bound of distribution range on volume
Confirmation candle closes below distribution low
exit_conditions: Stop loss at highest high of distribution phase + 1 ATR
Take profit at 2x risk
Exit if price reverses back into distribution range on high volume

rule_text: A trader should initiate short positions when market sentiment shifts from optimism to conviction of fundamental deterioration, evidenced by declining bounce heights, accelerating selling pressure, and exhaustion of bullish conviction.
entry_conditions: Price makes a new lower low
Bounce fails to reach 80% of previous bounce high
Volume on decline exceeds volume on bounce by 1.5x or more
Confirmed for at least 3 successive bounce cycles
exit_conditions: Price closes above previous bounce high
Time stop at 20 bars
Stop loss at 2x average true range above entry

rule_text: A trader should validate that fractal patterns remain tradable on their chosen timeframe by confirming sufficient liquidity exists; patterns degenerate into noise when liquidity is insufficient, making them unreliable for trading decisions.
entry_conditions: Fractal pattern identified on target timeframe
Instrument average volume (last 20 periods) exceeds 75th percentile of historical distribution
Bid-ask spread is in tightest 25% of recent range
Pattern confirmation occurs on higher timeframe simultaneously
exit_conditions: Exit if volume drops below 50th percentile during trade
Exit if spread widens beyond 50th percentile of range
Exit on pattern invalidation

Trend Continuation
Trend
rule_text: Enter trades in the direction of an established or nascent trend by using pullbacks within that trend to position for continuation of the trend leg.
entry_conditions: Trend is visible (price has made at least 2 confirmed higher highs and higher lows, or lower lows and lower highs)
Pullback retraces 38.2-50% of the prior trend leg
Price begins to reverse in the direction of trend (bodies forming in trend direction)

parameters.entry_proximity_to_trend_direction: reversal bar or close in trend direction

exit_conditions: Stop loss approximately 2ATR below pullback low (uptrend) or above pullback high (downtrend)
Trail stop as trend extends, or exit on trend structure violation

Trend Continuation
Trend
rule_text: Enter trades in the direction of an established or nascent trend by using pullbacks within that trend to position for continuation of the trend leg.
entry_conditions: Trend is visible or nascent (at least 1 higher high and higher low, or 1 lower low and lower high)
Pullback retraces 23.6-38.2% of the prior trend leg
Price shows any reversal signal in the direction of the trend (single reversal bar, or touch of pullback support)

parameters.entry_proximity_to_trend_direction: first reversal signal or support touch

exit_conditions: Stop loss tight (1ATR or swing low for uptrend; swing high for downtrend)
Quick take-profit on first momentum extension, or tight trailing stop

rule_text: A trader should exit or reduce conviction in early trend trades when price action contradicts the expected trend direction, treating failed breakouts as potential reversals rather than continuing to hold stubborn positions.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above accumulation into potential uptrend
Position size is small (1-2% risk per trade)

Any reversal wick below entry + 1 ATR

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch. P1:OTA, pp. 47-62
raw_chunk_text:
Trend continuation plays tend to be high-probability plays because there is a ver-
ifiable, statistical edge for trend continuation; these plays are aligned with one of the
fundamental principles of p
rule_text: A trader should enter trend continuation trades only when the risk/reward ratio is favorable, defined by identifying the precise price point at which the trend is conclusively violated and ensuring this point is close enough to entry to offer attractive risk/reward characteristics.
entry_conditions: Trend is confirmed in effect (price higher lows in uptrend or lower highs in downtrend)
Violation point can be defined at a recent swing low (uptrend) or swing high (downtrend)
Distance from entry to violation point ≤ 1.0% of entry price
Potential profit target ≥ 5.0× the stop-loss distance
exit_conditions: Price closes below/above the defined trend violation point
Profit target achieved (5× risk)

rule_text: A trader should take profits consistently at or just beyond previous resistance levels in trend continuation trades, rather than holding for extended moves, because most reliable gains occur at these defined levels while most failed continuation trades provide polite exits.
entry_conditions: Price pulls back within an established uptrend
Price resumes upward movement toward previous swing high
Volume supports continued trend pressure
exit_conditions: Close at or within 0 pips of previous swing high
Exit immediately upon reaching profit target
Stop loss: below recent swing low by 1.5x ATR

rule_text: A trader should reduce or exit positions when an obvious trend continuation play becomes extremely overextended and crowded with market participants, as reversals can be dramatic when everyone attempts to exit simultaneously.
entry_conditions: Identify established trend with obvious continuation pattern
Measure crowding: percentile of open interest or positioning at top 75th percentile or higher
Confirm overextension: price beyond 2.0 standard deviations from mean
exit_conditions: Close 50% of position when both crowding AND overextension detected
Exit remaining 50% on first reversal signal (close below 20-period MA) or after 5 bars

Do not hold for reversal; accept termination as profit

Exit on stop loss if overextension reverses immediately

Exit on tick-by-tick stop if momentum doesn't reverse

rule_text: Position long on lower timeframe pullbacks within an uptrend, targeting trend continuation with asymmetric risk/reward where winning trades offer larger rewards than initial risk, accepting lower probability for higher payoff potential.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe is in confirmed uptrend (price above MA200)
Lower timeframe pullback has retraced 38.2%-50% of prior leg high
Price forms support structure or mean reversion signal on lower timeframe
Risk-to-reward ratio is at least 1:2.5
exit_conditions: Stop loss at recent pullback low + 1 ATR
Target at prior swing high or higher timeframe resistance
Exit if higher timeframe uptrend breaks

rule_text: A trader must reduce position size and enforce strict risk controls when trading countertrend positions in parabolic/manic market conditions to prevent career-ending losses from gap moves overnight.

Position is countertrend to primary direction
Overnight holding is required for swing trade
exit_conditions: Stop-loss hit at 1.0x ATR
Parabolic condition ends (volatility normalizes)
End of trading session if overnight gap risk unacceptable

rule_text: Failed breakouts from support/resistance levels are high-probability trades because they occur when market participants are positioned incorrectly, creating dramatic move potential in the opposite direction.
entry_conditions: Price breaks beyond support/resistance by N% or more (true breakout attempt)
Price closes back inside the original support/resistance level (failed breakout confirmed)
Wait for one additional confirmation candle in the reversal direction
Volume on rejection candle exceeds volume on breakout attempt candle
exit_conditions: Stop loss above the failed breakout high (or below if support rejection)
Take profit at prior support/resistance level or 2:1 risk/reward minimum

Setup matches pre-defined technical rules in trading plan
exit_conditions: Exit per predefined plan rule
Stop loss at max 2% account risk

entry_conditions: Trend established with multiple bars of consistent direction
Pullback extends for 6+ bars with sharp counter-trend momentum
Momentum indicator (MACD or RSI) reaches 3+ SD extreme opposite trend
exit_conditions: Exit only after momentum spike persists 3+ bars at extreme AND price fails to resume trend

rule_text: A trader should buy pullbacks following upward impulse moves or short bounces after downward impulse moves, but must be cautious when the impulse move is extremely strong, as this signals potential climax and reversal rather than continuation.
entry_conditions: Impulse move (new momentum high/low) completed
Pullback/bounce in progress
Impulse move magnitude < 2.0 standard deviations from 20-period average
No strong countertrend momentum on pullback (RSI < 60 on up pullback, RSI > 40 on down bounce)
Subsequent trend leg shows similar impulse structure
exit_conditions: Exit if price reverses more than 50% of pullback without follow-through
Exit if countertrend momentum exceeds 70 RSI (up moves) or 30 RSI (down moves)

rule_text: Recognize buying climaxes marked by emotional mania and exhaustion as vulnerable reversal points; a trader should prepare to fade or exit long positions when price action shows extreme buying intensity followed by the slightest pause or loss of momentum, as the last buyer has entered and the market is vulnerable to sharp sell-offs.
entry_conditions: Price has rallied for 5+ consecutive strong up days with volume expansion
Latest bar shows a pause, doji, or small up close (diminishing momentum)
Close breaks below the low of the climax bar or below a key support level
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 61.8% retracement of the climax move
Exit on close back above the climax high
Time stop after 3 bars if target not hit

rule_text: A trader should identify and prepare to trade reversals when price exhibits climactic behavior: steep trends with expanded bar ranges, intraday volatility spikes, volume 4-5x average, and bars closing at extremes (highs in buying climax, lows in selling climax).
entry_conditions: Bar range > 2.5x SMA(20) of bar range
Volume > 4x SMA(20) of volume
Close > 75th percentile of bar (within top 25% of bar range for buying climax)
3+ consecutive bars meeting these criteria
Price distance from moving average > 2 standard deviations
exit_conditions: Exit on first close below entry price or 1 ATR profit target

At least 2 additional confirmation candles form after climax (not just pullback)
exit_conditions: Do not enter during the 5-bar window immediately following climax detection

rule_text: A trader should avoid initiating short positions into pullbacks that follow a selling climax, as the climax itself is a warning signal of exhaustion and potential trend reversal.
entry_conditions: Selling climax identified: price parabolic expansion below lower Bollinger Band
Large range bars closing on lows present (≥3 bars)
Free bars (highs below lower band) present (≥2 bars)
Pullback begins (price moves higher for 1+ bars)
New higher low formed relative to climax low
exit_conditions: Exit avoided short if entry was prevented
If mistakenly short, cover on close above 20-period moving average

rule_text: Avoid trading in the direction of blow-off climax moves by identifying characteristic climactic patterns and remaining sensitive to the potential for misdirection, adjusting positions quickly when error is detected.
entry_conditions: Price has moved extreme distance in one direction over recent periods
Volume spike or acceleration observed during final leg of move

No short-term pullback or consolidation in final 5-10% of move
exit_conditions: Exit immediately; do not enter trades aligned with climax direction
If already holding directional position, exit on first lower high or momentum divergence

rule_text: After a non-climactic impulse move, enter trades in the direction of the original trend during the pullback phase, when volume is lighter and price retraces against the impulse.
entry_conditions: Identify completed non-climactic impulse move
Price retraces to 38.2% Fibonacci level of impulse
Volume during pullback is 40-50% below impulse move volume
Price closes above pullback low on expanding volume
RSI or momentum indicator confirms directional bias
exit_conditions: Exit at pullback high + 1 ATR
Exit on close below pullback low
Time-based exit at 5-bar hold if no trend resumption

rule_text: Assess the strength of buyer commitment during pullbacks in an uptrend by measuring pullback depth and structure; shallow pullbacks with simple structure signal aggressive accumulation, while deeper pullbacks with complex structure signal buyer uncertainty and weakening commitment.
entry_conditions: Price in established uptrend (higher highs and higher lows)
Pullback retraces ≤20% of prior advance measured peak-to-current
Pullback structure is simple (single leg down, no multiple legs)
exit_conditions: Pullback exceeds 38.2% of prior advance
Pullback develops complex multi-leg structure
Price closes below prior swing low

Minimum 2-period confirmation after structure completion
exit_conditions: Exit at highest high during pullback formation (hard stop)
Exit if pullback structure is violated by price closing beyond pullback extreme

rule_text: In a trending market, pullbacks alternate between simple and complex structures; a trader should expect and prepare for the opposite pullback type after each occurrence.
entry_conditions: Trend is established (price > 20-period EMA)
Simple pullback has completed (retracement ≤ 50% of prior leg)
New trend leg initiates above prior pullback high
Expect next pullback will be complex (>50% retracement) when it occurs
exit_conditions: Exit if pullback violates expected structure after 2+ legs confirmed
Exit on trend line break
Scale out at resistance if complex pullback extends beyond 78% retracement

Pullback has reversed direction and closed beyond first-leg extreme

Exit if pullback retraces more than 50% of prior trend leg without confirming reversal

rule_text: When price makes multiple pushes to new highs with increasing frequency (suggesting buyer desperation), a trader should tighten stops and prepare to fade the trend, as this pattern reliably precedes trend termination.
entry_conditions: Identify 3 sequential pushes to new highs within an uptrend
Each push separated by fewer than 8 bars
Each successive push reaches higher high than previous
Volume/volatility shows acceleration across the three pushes
exit_conditions: Tighten stop-loss to breakeven or recent swing low
Exit on close below the middle push high
Do NOT initiate new short positions

Momentum divergence or support at pullback level confirmed

Exit if impulse leg fails to break prior highs/lows within 3 bars

raw_chunk_text:
Follows Good Momentum or Impulse When trading pullbacks in a trend, it is
helpful to make sure the market is actually trending. One of the best ways to separate
out the suboptimal trading environments
rule_text: Trade pullbacks only when preceded by significant momentum in the direction of the trend, measured by the magnitude of the prior move relative to previous swings.
entry_conditions: Market is in an established trend (price above 200-period MA)
Pullback occurs (price retraces into support/resistance zone)
Preceding impulse move is 1.5x the amplitude of the three prior swings in same direction
Volume on impulse move exceeds average volume by 20%
exit_conditions: Price closes below the pullback entry level plus 2 ATR
Pullback retraces more than 61.8% of the preceding impulse

rule_text: A trader should size positions so that no single trade can eliminate their trading capital, and should avoid adding to losing trades during strong trend moves, since extended moves are rare but catastrophic when they occur against stubborn positions.
entry_conditions: Trade identified in strong confirmed trend (pullbacks are notably smaller than with-trend legs)
Initial position sized to risk max 2% of account on stop loss
Additional units may be added on each retracement below 62% of prior with-trend leg
exit_conditions: Exit at stop loss if loss reaches 2% of account
Exit at retracement of 75% of prior with-trend leg
Partial exits at 50% and 62% retracement levels to lock in trend gains

rule_text: A trader should enter pullback trades either at predetermined retracement levels with large stops and small position sizes, or wait for lower timeframe and price action confirmation that the pullback termination has occurred.
entry_conditions: Price retraces 25-75% of preceding setup leg
Lower timeframe (e.g., 1/5 min vs 15/60 min chart) shows reversal price action
Price action confirmation includes rejection candle or swing low formation
Risk/reward ratio minimum 1:2 achievable with confirmation distance
exit_conditions: Exit at 2:1 risk/reward target
Exit if price breaks below pullback low without confirmation structure
Exit if setup leg high is broken

rule_text: A trader should avoid or reduce position sizing in extremely strong trends that show shallow or absent pullbacks, because the lack of advantageous entry prices and spike volatility against trend direction create unfavorable risk/reward conditions.
entry_conditions: Strong uptrend identified (price above 20-period MA, higher highs/lows)
Recent pullback is <23.6% of prior swing (Fibonacci shallow threshold)
Current price within 1 ATR of recent high
DECISION: Do not enter
exit_conditions: N/A — no position taken

rule_text: A trader should identify pullbacks by monitoring lower time frame price action for reduced conviction and lower volatility, as pullbacks are consolidation periods characterized by lower activity compared to trend legs.
entry_conditions: Primary trend established (higher highs/lows on daily or weekly)
Lower time frame volatility (ATR or Bollinger Band width) drops to bottom 20th percentile of 20-period lookback
Price consolidates within 30% of the trend leg amplitude
Lower time frame shows reduced volume or momentum divergence
exit_conditions: Exit on break of consolidation range (trend continuation signal)
Exit if lower time frame conviction returns (ATR/vol re-expands above 40th percentile)

rule_text: A trader should anticipate pullbacks in lower timeframes during strong trends by monitoring for reduced conviction and smaller ranges, as these pullbacks often exhibit symmetry on the trading timeframe before price resumes the prevailing trend direction.
entry_conditions: Price in confirmed trend on trading timeframe (e.g., above 20-period EMA)
Lower timeframe pullback range is ≤40% of prior trend leg range
Pullback high-to-low symmetry ratio between 0.95–1.05 on trading timeframe
No spike exceeding previous pullback extremes by more than 1 ATR
Volume on pullback candles ≤50% of trend-move volume
exit_conditions: Price closes below entry + 2 ATR support zone
Pullback structure breaks symmetry by >10% before resumption

rule_text: Trade retracements that exhibit consistent geometric patterns (angle, depth, symmetry) with higher confidence than erratic or inconsistent retracements; occasional spikes outside pattern boundaries may offer entries but should not dominate the trade plan.
entry_conditions: Identify prior retracement cycles (minimum 3 occurrences)
Measure angle of each retracement (in degrees from trend)
Measure depth of each retracement (as % of preceding move)
Calculate coefficient of variation across angles and depths
Enter only when both angle and depth variance < 15%
Exclude entries coinciding with spikes >2 standard deviations beyond pattern
exit_conditions: Exit at prior retracement extreme (symmetry breakout)
Exit if angle deviates >20% from established pattern
Stop loss: 1.5x average prior retracement depth

rule_text: A trader should identify and exit losing trades early by recognizing warning signs that precede pullback failures, rather than holding through dramatic reversals that shake out positions.
entry_conditions: Long position established in trending market
Pullback initiated (price retraces into support zone)
First warning signal detected: volatility spike, amplitude exceeding prior pullbacks by >15%, or volume divergence
exit_conditions: Exit at first warning sign of impending failure
Maximum loss: 0.5% account risk

rule_text: Avoid trading in flat consolidation ranges that emerge from failed pullbacks, as price action becomes random and breakouts are prone to fakeouts. Exit or avoid such patterns once identified.
entry_conditions: Pullback leg identified (retracement ≥38.2% and ≤61.8% of prior trend)
Price action fails to break prior swing extreme within 5 bars
Range width (high-low over last N bars) falls below 0.5 × average pullback range
exit_conditions: Close position immediately upon flat consolidation pattern recognition
Do not re-enter until trend resumes with new swing extreme break

rule_text: Enter with-trend trades when a consolidation pattern within an established trend fails to break in the counter-trend direction, as this traps traders on the wrong side and creates a high-probability continuation opportunity.
entry_conditions: Established uptrend (price above 50-period MA)
Consolidation/pullback forms with defined lower boundary
Two or more large candlesticks fail to close below consolidation low
Volume on failure candles exceeds 150% of consolidation average
Price reclaims consolidation midpoint before entry
exit_conditions: Close below consolidation low + 1 ATR
Time stop: 20 bars without new trend high

rule_text: A trader should identify and enter trending markets by recognizing persistent directional price movement (higher highs and higher lows in uptrends, or lower lows and lower highs in downtrends) across the chart structure, selecting entry timing based on their risk tolerance and trend maturity preference.

Close below/above previous swing low/high

rule_text: A valid uptrend requires BOTH higher pivot highs AND higher pivot lows; a valid downtrend requires BOTH lower lows AND lower highs. Incomplete structure (e.g., higher highs without higher lows) indicates weak control and reduced trend reliability.
entry_conditions: Identify 3 consecutive higher pivot highs
Confirm 3 consecutive higher pivot lows accompanying each HH
All 6 pivots (3 HH + 3 HL) must form unbroken sequence
No lower low between any HH-HL pair
exit_conditions: First break of the most recent higher low (downtrend: first break of most recent lower high)
Failure to confirm next expected pivot in sequence

Price fails to exceed the previous pivot high (uptrend) or pivot low (downtrend) by a buffer of 0.5% or greater

No re-entry until price makes a new extreme that exceeds previous pivot by >1.0%

rule_text: A trader should buy breakouts in the direction of the existing trend during complex consolidations, rather than shorting them, because these patterns frequently precede exceptional trade opportunities in the trend direction.

exit_conditions: Exit long position entirely or reduce by 50%

rule_text: A trader should monitor for a downswing in an uptrend that is longer than the preceding upswing, as it signals a potential change in market character and buyer support failure, which may set up a countertrend short opportunity on the next bounce.
entry_conditions: Current downswing length > preceding upswing length by at least 1.5x
Downswing creates a lower low vs prior swing
Price bounces back up from the swing low
Lower timeframe (1/4 of primary timeframe) shows rejection at bounce high
Volume or price action confirms selling pressure dominance
exit_conditions: Exit above the swing high that preceded the down move
Or profit target at 1.0x the downswing length

exit_conditions: Trend line is definitively broken (close beyond line + buffer)

rule_text: A trendline should only be drawn between successive market swings; if the price action shows no identifiable swings, no trendline should be constructed.
entry_conditions: Price has completed 3 consecutive, non-overlapping swings
Each swing minimum amplitude = 2% of current price level
Trendline touches at least 3 swing points with no price body penetration
Swing duration minimum = 5 bars
exit_conditions: Price closes below/above trendline by more than 1.5% of average recent range
Swing structure breaks (fewer than 2 consecutive swings visible)

rule_text: Trendlines drawn on flat markets without genuine price swings lack significance and are easily penetrated; a trader should validate that authentic swings exist before using a trendline as a trading signal.

Trendline has been tested and held at least 3 times without penetration
Price approaches trendline with clear directional momentum (not ranging)
exit_conditions: Trendline penetrated by close below/above line
Close + 1 ATR(14) moves beyond line

Price breaks above upper channel line → exit remaining position
Trend structure breaks (lower low below lower channel) → exit all

rule_text: A trader should reduce exposure and book partial profits when price pauses at channel boundaries, while monitoring for consolidation-breakout patterns that signal strong directional conviction and accelerating trends.
entry_conditions: Price approaches and pauses within 0.5% of parallel channel boundary
Volume contracts below 50th percentile of recent 20 bars
Price consolidates for minimum 3 bars within 1% of channel boundary
Breakout bar closes beyond channel with 75th percentile volume confirmation
exit_conditions: Exit 50% of position immediately upon pause at channel boundary
Exit remaining position if price reverses back into channel after 2 bars
Exit if consolidation extends beyond 5 bars without breakout

exit_conditions: Exit 25% of position on approach to channel boundary

Allow consolidation to extend up to 8 bars before exiting

rule_text: A trader should monitor lower timeframe price action around critical levels in real time and exit losing positions quickly if the expected immediate directional move does not materialize, using responsiveness to this intrabar information to reduce losses and reposition correctly.
entry_conditions: Position established at critical support/resistance level
Begin monitoring 1-2 timeframes lower than decision timeframe in real time
Establish expected direction based on price action setup
exit_conditions: Within 3 bars of lower timeframe, if expected directional meltdown/surge does not begin, exit position
Or if price moves against position by 0.5% without confirming direction, exit immediately

rule_text: A trader should use two-day trendlines (drawn from two days prior's low to yesterday's high) to identify overextended market conditions and time entries at exhaustion points, but only after developing sufficient skill to avoid analysis paralysis from excessive trendline redrawing.
entry_conditions: Two-day trendline drawn from (Low[T-2] to High[T-1]) is extended into current period
Price has moved 2+ ATR beyond the trendline (confirming overextension)
Trader has minimum 6 months of charting experience and established discipline rules
Market is in a clear trending regime (ADX > 25)
Volume confirms move away from trendline
exit_conditions: Exit at next swing high/low or when price reverses through trendline by 1 ATR
Exit if overextension does not reverse within 5 bars

rule_text: A trader should identify potential reversal or inflection points by drawing trend lines between two prior bars and observing where those lines intersect the current price level, exploiting the tendency of markets to oscillate within geometrically-definable volatility bands.
entry_conditions: Trend line drawn from 2 bars prior (day before yesterday high to yesterday low)
Current bar price intersects this trend line
Intersection occurs within 0.5 ATR of the line
Volume on intersection bar ≥ 20-day average volume
RSI(14) shows divergence at the intersection point
exit_conditions: Exit on close beyond opposite trend line extreme by 1 ATR
Time-based exit: close position at end of next bar if inflection fails

rule_text: A trader should enter a position when short-term trendlines are violated during pullbacks, as the recovery from the break often catalyzes a move in the opposite direction of the initial break.
entry_conditions: Short-term trendline defined by 3+ consecutive bars in pullback
Price closes beyond trendline
Price recovers and closes 2+ bars back above (long) or below (short) the violated trendline
Recovery candle closes with conviction (>50% of candle body beyond line)
exit_conditions: Price reverses back through the original trendline
Stop loss at the extreme of the pullback

rule_text: When a trendline is broken, the original trend often continues at a shallower slope rather than reversing; a trader should redraw the trendline at the new rate and use the updated line as the relevant support/resistance level, not treat the break as a trend reversal signal.

Price retraces and retests the new trendline
Price holds above the new trendline on retest
exit_conditions: Price closes decisively below the new trendline (>1 close)
Break below new trendline with volume confirmation

rule_text: Treat support and resistance as probabilistic zones rather than deterministic lines, and remain flexible to price action signals at the hard right edge instead of rigidly expecting bounces at predetermined levels.
entry_conditions: Price approaches support/resistance zone (defined as ±2.0% band around identified level)
Price demonstrates pause behavior (closes within zone for 2+ consecutive bars)
Secondary confirmation signal present (divergence, volume, or momentum indicator)
exit_conditions: Price closes outside zone on close
Secondary signal reverses
2% profit target OR 3 bars elapsed without further confirmation

rule_text: Treat support and resistance as zones rather than precise lines, and anticipate price spikes through broken levels followed by renewed rejection at the original level—account for this behavior in risk and trade management.
entry_conditions: Price approaches identified support or resistance zone
Zone is significant and widely watched (confirmed by volume/prior tests)
Trade only on rejection INTO the zone, not on approach
exit_conditions: Exit immediately if price spikes through zone by 5+ pips without holding
Exit if stop is hit beyond the extended zone
Take profit if price rejects and reverses into the zone

rule_text: A broken support or resistance level reverses its role—broken support becomes resistance and broken resistance becomes support—due to trapped traders covering positions at breakeven, creating predictable price rejection at these pivotal levels.
entry_conditions: Price broke above resistance level (confirmed by close)
Price has moved at least 2% higher after the break
Price has returned to within 0.5% of broken resistance
Price closes BELOW the broken resistance level (rejection confirmed)
Volume at rejection is >= 90th percentile of recent volume
exit_conditions: Take profit: 2% below broken resistance level
Stop loss: 1% above broken resistance level

Volume on retest is lower than break volume

Time-based: Exit if no movement within 5–10 bars

rule_text: A trader should be skeptical of price action at support/resistance levels that appear to fail, as shallow penetrations are often traps designed to liquidate trapped traders before the true move occurs in the opposite direction.

Enter reversal trade only after price closes back through the level

Exit if hold is broken and level re-tested with strong volume

Price approaches level from expected direction
exit_conditions: Level is violated by more than 1.0% of level value
Position held at level for 20+ bars without direction confirmation

exit_conditions: Exit if price fails to sustain back through support/resistance
Exit at break-even if reversal pattern fails within 5 bars
Take profit at prior swing high/low in direction of accumulation/distribution

rule_text: A large buyer should scale into purchases by holding back initial demand when price approaches the target, then accelerate buying aggressively only when price threatens to break below the target and other buyers show strength, to minimize chase-buying at higher prices.

Price rallies 1% above re-entry point without sustained volume

rule_text: A trader should approach trading ranges with the expectation that they represent continuation patterns of the preceding trend, positioning within a higher timeframe structure rather than trading within the range itself.
entry_conditions: Price has formed a trading range (2+ touches of support and resistance)
Range duration is 5+ bars on trading timeframe
Preceding trend on higher timeframe (4H or daily) is clearly established (20+ bars trending)
Price approaches either edge of range with volume confirmation
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks outside range in direction opposite to preceding trend
Exit if range breaks down in volatility (closes below/above range with high volume rejection)
Profit target at 1.5x range width from breakout point

rule_text: When trading noise on the focus timeframe appears unreadable, consult a higher timeframe to determine if the noise is a continuation pullback or a reversal signal; treat ranges as continuation patterns (innocent until proven guilty) and use higher timeframe context to distinguish signal from noise.
entry_conditions: Focus timeframe enters large trading range after extended trend
Higher timeframe (e.g., weekly) shows trend in same direction as prior focus timeframe trend
Higher timeframe RSI > 70 (overbought) OR price > 2-standard-deviation band above MA
Range duration on focus timeframe ≥ 10 bars
Higher timeframe still above 20-period MA
exit_conditions: Price breaks range on focus timeframe in direction of higher timeframe trend
Higher timeframe closes below 20-period MA
Range extends beyond 3x initial range size

rule_text: A trader should prepare to enter countertrend trades when price reaches overextended levels on higher timeframes while daily price action shows lack of conviction at those extremes, positioning for mean reversion when a shock disrupts the unsustainable move.
entry_conditions: Weekly RSI > 70 or price > 2 std dev above 20-week MA
Daily shows 2+ consecutive doji, pin bar, or inside bar patterns at the high
Daily volume declining on the final push higher
No new daily highs in 3+ bars
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1.5 ATR above entry
Target: 50% retracement of the weekly overextended move
Exit if daily closes above entry with volume confirmation

exit_conditions: Price reverses back into original range
New range boundary fails to hold

rule_text: A trader should initiate entries when springs (false breaks below support in uptrends) or upthrusts (false breaks above resistance in downtrends) occur near the edges of consolidation patterns, as these offer high-probability entries into higher timeframe trends.
entry_conditions: Consolidation range formed for minimum 8 bars
Internal bar range <= 0.5 ATR of prior 20 bars
Spring/upthrust penetrates support/resistance by <= 1 ATR
Price retraces 50-75% back into consolidation range
Higher timeframe trend confirmed by 50-period moving average direction
exit_conditions: Exit at opposite edge of consolidation if spring/upthrust fails
Exit at 2x risk stop if higher timeframe trend reverses

rule_text: A trader should initiate entries when springs (false breaks below support in uptrends) or upthrusts (false breaks above resistance in downtrends) occur near the edges of consolidation patterns, as these offer high-probability entries into higher timeframe trends.

Higher timeframe trend confirmed by 20-period moving average direction
exit_conditions: Exit at opposite edge of consolidation range
Trail stop at 1.5x risk behind most recent swing low/high

rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades when price exhibits volatility compression (range contraction) followed by directional breakout, with ascending triangles showing particular strength due to progressively higher lows into resistance.

Volume on breakout candle > 1.5x 20-day average volume
exit_conditions: Close below breakout candle low
Time-based: 20 bars with no new highs
Profit target: 3x initial risk

rule_text: Avoid trading during consolidation periods that follow large opposing spike moves, as these environments generate random oscillations and frequent losses.
entry_conditions: Identify initial spike move: price move >= 3.0% of recent range in one direction within 1-2 bars
Confirm reversal spike: price move >= 2.5% in opposite direction within 1-2 bars following initial spike
Verify triangle formation emerging: Bollinger Band width contracting to <= 50% of pre-spike width
exit_conditions: Flat position; no new entries during triangle phase
Re-entry only after breakout confirmed: price closes beyond upper/lower BB by 1.5x ATR

rule_text: Avoid trading during consolidation periods that follow large opposing spike moves, as these environments generate random oscillations and frequent losses.
entry_conditions: Identify initial spike move: price move >= 2.5% of recent range in one direction within 1-3 bars

Verify triangle formation: ATR contracted to <= 60% of pre-spike ATR
exit_conditions: Reduce open position size to 50% of pre-pattern size

Exit remaining position on triangle breakout beyond 1.2x ATR from midpoint

rule_text: Avoid trading during consolidation periods that follow large opposing spike moves, as these environments generate random oscillations and frequent losses.
entry_conditions: Identify initial spike move: price move >= 2.0% of recent range in one direction within 1-4 bars
Confirm reversal spike: price move >= 1.5% in opposite direction within 2-4 bars following initial spike
Verify triangle formation: Bollinger Band width contracted to <= 70% of pre-spike width OR price oscillating within defined bands
exit_conditions: Enter fade trades (small size) at BB extremes during triangle oscillations
Exit each fade trade at opposite BB band or at 0.75x ATR profit
Stop-loss on each fade: 0.5x ATR (tight hard stops)
Exit all remaining positions on breakout beyond 1.0x ATR from midpoint

rule_text: Avoid trading during expanding ranges or diamond formations where volatility is widening and price action is non-directional, because risk control and position management become difficult.
entry_conditions: Detect expanding range: each successive swing high exceeds prior swing high by ATR threshold
Detect expanding range: each successive swing low is lower than prior swing low by ATR threshold
Confirm non-directional: ADX < 20 (weak trend)
exit_conditions: Exit ALL open positions immediately upon expanding range detection

rule_text: A trader should enter in the direction of an established trend when a support or resistance level that has been tested multiple times is finally broken, as pent-up pressure may drive a sharp continuation.
entry_conditions: Support or resistance level tested and rejected at least 4+ times
Price closes beyond level by at least 0.5% of recent range
Volume on breakout exceeds 120% of 20-period average
Trend direction confirmed by price above/below 50-period moving average
exit_conditions: Close back below broken level on daily timeframe
Risk-based stop at previous swing low before level break

rule_text: The best breakout trades are those driven by high institutional interest and involvement, which increases the probability of successful price continuation beyond support or resistance levels.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level or below support level
Volume on breakout bar exceeds 150% of 20-period average volume
Breakout occurs with price closing beyond level (not just intrabar touch)
At least 2 prior rejection attempts at resistance/support evident
exit_conditions: Close back below breakout level
Price retraces 50% of initial breakout move

rule_text: Avoid trading breakouts at the point of actual breakout execution, as large institutional traders who are already positioned will use the volatility to distribute their positions to retail traders, causing failed breakouts. Instead, identify when institutional positioning has already occurred and trade after the initial retail rush has absorbed their supply.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level
At least 3 consecutive closes above resistance (not just first touch)
Volume on breakout bar is below 75th percentile of recent volume (indicating retail has not yet rushed in)
No entry within first 2 bars of initial breakout
exit_conditions: Close below the breakout level
Stop loss at lowest point of prior consolidation

Instead, identify when institutional positioning has already occurred and trade after the initial retail rush has absorbed their supply.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level
Breakout confirmed with close above resistance on same or next bar
Volume spike on breakout bar exceeds 90th percentile of recent 20-bar volume (retail panic buying evident)
RSI or momentum indicator shows overbought conditions (60+) on breakout
Enter on pullback toward breakout level after initial spike exhausts
exit_conditions: Take profit at 2x the initial resistance-to-entry range
Stop loss 0.5% below breakout level

Short position: sell into this buying pressure, anticipating supply from institutional distribution
exit_conditions: Take profit at 50-75% of the institutional unwind (estimated via volume profile)
Stop loss 1% above breakout high
Time-based exit if position not profitable within 5 bars

rule_text: Large informed traders reveal their accumulation intentions through higher lows into resistance; a trader should initiate long positions when price makes successively higher lows approaching a resistance level, as this pattern indicates institutional buying pressure before a breakout.
entry_conditions: Price has formed at least 4 successively higher lows
Each higher low is within 0.5 ATR of the previous low (tight progression)
Clearly defined resistance level is visible (tested 2+ times)
Price approaches within 1% of resistance
Volume on higher lows is below 50th percentile (accumulation signature)
exit_conditions: Close below the lowest of the higher low series
Resistance broken with >2% penetration and volume surge
Time stop: 20 bars without breakout attempt

rule_text: A trader should increase buying aggression when observing shallow market declines and evidence of competing large buyers entering the market, as this signals building institutional demand that will likely arrest further downside and force prices higher.
entry_conditions: Market declines from recent high
Decline amplitude is < 38.2% of prior move
Price closes above decline low on 2+ consecutive bars
Volume on recovery exceeds volume on decline
exit_conditions: Exit if decline exceeds 50% retracement of prior move
Exit if close below decline low on high volume

Take profit: 50% of larger range height from entry

rule_text: Identify accumulation or distribution activity by detecting springs and upthrusts in pre-breakout ranges, as these patterns reveal institutional positioning before major moves.
entry_conditions: Price action forms a spring (dip below range low) or upthrust (spike above range high)
Spring/upthrust retraces back into range within 2 candles
Volume on spring/upthrust is >150% of 20-period average
Range has persisted for minimum 20 bars
Wait for close back within range + 1 additional confirmation candle before entry
exit_conditions: Exit on close below range low if breakout fails
Profit target at 1.5x range width from breakout point

rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades when price violates support/resistance with short-lived excursions (springs/upthrusts) that reverse back into the range, as this signals institutional positioning and high-probability breakout setups.
entry_conditions: Price violates support or resistance by >= 2.0% of range width
Excursion lasts <= 2 bars (short-lived requirement)
Price reverses and closes back inside the range by >= 1.5% of range width
Volume on violation bar is >= 1.5x baseline average
Wait for second test of range edge before entering breakout direction
exit_conditions: Stop loss at low of spring/upthrust violation bar
Take profit at 2.5x the risk (risk/reward minimum 1:2.5)

rule_text: A breakout is high-quality only when it is accompanied by unusual volume, activity, and order flow intensity; gentle price movement through resistance without corresponding volume increase signals a weak or false breakout.

Price closes above resistance (not just intraday touch)
No signs of accumulation tightness immediately before breakout
exit_conditions: Close below resistance level
Volume drops below 150% of average on continuation
Tightening price action without continued volume support

rule_text: A valid breakout that initiates an extended trend is characterized by increased volatility measured as the expansion of individual bar ranges relative to recent price action history.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above (or below) established support/resistance level
Current bar range (high - low) exceeds 1.5x the average range of the prior 20 bars
Increased volume accompanies the price breakout
exit_conditions: Price reverses back through breakout level with volume
Volatility compression below 1.2x the 20-bar average for 3+ consecutive bars

rule_text: A trader should view slippage at breakout points as a positive signal of genuine market movement rather than purely as a cost, and should expect to pay higher prices when entering strong breakouts.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above established resistance level
Actual fill slippage is less than 0.5% of breakout price
Volume on breakout bar exceeds 20-day average volume by at least 50%
exit_conditions: Exit if price reverses below breakout level + (2 × slippage amount)
Exit on profit target or trailing stop based on breakout magnitude

rule_text: Positive slippage (receiving a better-than-expected fill price) on a breakout entry is a warning signal that the breakout is likely to fail; a trader should reduce position size or cancel the trade when receiving unexpectedly favorable fill prices on breakout orders.

Actual fill received at price better than expected
exit_conditions: Exit full position immediately upon detecting positive slippage > 5 bps from expected price

Actual fill received at price better than expected by 10+ bps
exit_conditions: Reduce position size by 50% when positive slippage detected
Exit remaining position if breakout fails within 2-3 bars

rule_text: A trader should establish positions in the direction of a breakout during the first pullback after the initial thrust, as this is the optimal entry point if the breakout will sustain, and the pullback's character reveals conviction behind the move.
entry_conditions: Price breaks through established level with sharp thrust beyond level
Price pulls back toward breakout level
Price holds at least 50% of the breakout thrust distance beyond breakout level
Pullback shows strong rejection candle or multiple closes above breakout level
Volume on pullback reversal exceeds volume on initial breakout thrust
exit_conditions: Price closes back below original breakout level on strong volume
Price fails to make higher highs within 5 bars of entry

rule_text: A trader should empirically validate whether breakout patterns that hold cleanly beyond the breakout level actually produce superior trend extension compared to breakouts that fail to hold, rather than assuming they do.
entry_conditions: Price breaks defined support/resistance level
Historical backtest shows clean holds outperform failed holds by at least 15% in average trend extension
Sample size of 500+ trades analyzed
exit_conditions: Trend extension terminates or reverses
Price closes back inside breakout level

rule_text: A trader should wait for short-term traders to fully exit their losing positions below a breakout level before entering aggressively, as large players accumulate inventory during this capitulation phase before resuming the trend.
entry_conditions: Initial breakout level established and tested from below
Price pulls back 2-3% below breakout level
Volume dries up to 40% of breakout volume for at least 3 consecutive bars
Price reverses and closes back above breakout level with expansion of volume
exit_conditions: Close below breakout level on high volume
20-period moving average turns down

rule_text: A trader should not expect support/resistance levels to hold cleanly on pullbacks; anticipate that initial breakout level violations will trigger short-term trader exits, which then catalyze the real directional move. Plan entries accordingly to respect this market reality.
entry_conditions: Initial breakout above/below level occurs
Pullback re-tests the level from the correct side
Price holds above/below level on second test (does not violate again)
Volume on hold is greater than volume on initial violation
exit_conditions: Exit if level is broken again on pullback
Exit if price fails to hold on retest within 2 bars

Exit final tranche when volatility mean-reverts below 125%

Price reverses within 3-5 candles and approaches the level from outside
Volume on failure excursion is 40-70% of average volume
Enter within 0.2% of the significant level on retest
exit_conditions: Stop loss at penetration high/low by 0.5x ATR
Take profit at 1.5x risk or first intraday swing

rule_text: A trader should enter a reversion trade on a failure test (brief excursion beyond a breakout level followed by immediate reversal) when price closes back inside the breakout level within a limited number of bars, signaling weak breakout momentum.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level (upside breakout)
Price reverses and closes back INSIDE the level on bar 1 or bar 2
Close is strong (near high of reversal bar or better)
Volume or momentum indicator confirms reversal strength
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1.5x ATR above the failed breakout level
Take profit: 50% of prior trend move down
Time stop: Exit if price holds above level for 5 bars

rule_text: When a breakout fails and price reverses back below the breakout level with urgency-driven selling pressure, this creates a high-probability reversion opportunity due to trapped buyers exiting in panic.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above recent resistance level
Price closes back below breakout level within 3-4 bars
Volume on reversion bars is >150% of breakout bar volume
Close below breakout level confirmed on second bar below level
exit_conditions: Exit at 2x ATR below entry
Exit on close above breakout level
Time-based stop at 10 bars if profit target not hit

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch.5 (OTA), pp.137-162
rule_text: When a breakout fails and price reverses back below the breakout level with urgency-driven selling pressure, this creates a high-probability reversion opportunity due to trapped buyers exiting in panic.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above recent resistance level
Price closes back below breakout level within 2 bars
Volume on reversion bar is >120% of average volume
Close below breakout level with momentum indicator (RSI<50 or MACD negative)
exit_conditions: Exit at 1.5x ATR below entry
Exit on close above breakout level
Time-based stop at 7 bars if profit target not hit

rule_text: When a breakout fails and price reverses back below the breakout level with urgency-driven selling pressure, this creates a high-probability reversion opportunity due to trapped buyers exiting in panic.

exit_conditions: Exit at 1x ATR below entry
Exit on close above breakout level

rule_text: A trader should be skeptical of breakouts that lack conviction on lower timeframes, indicated by slow/reluctant bounces and easier downward movement than upward movement, as these signal likely breakout failures rather than trend continuations.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above/below level (primary timeframe)
On lower timeframe (e.g., 5-min bars during 1-hour breakout): observe 3+ consecutive slow bounces that fail to extend higher
Price drops through level with visibly less resistance than bounce attempts
Close price on breakout bar is within 25% of level distance from breakout level
exit_conditions: Exit fade trade if price extends beyond level with strong momentum bars (high volume, large range)
Exit at breakeven + slippage if conviction strengthens on lower timeframe

rule_text: A trader should anticipate mean-reversion momentum back inside a breakout level when price action shows weak conviction (asymmetric ease of movement) beyond that level, as this signals a failed breakout rather than a sustained move.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above a resistance level
Upward movement velocity is noticeably slower than downward retracements (measured by bar heights or close-to-close distance)
Asymmetric action persists for 4+ consecutive bars
Volume on up moves is lower than on down moves within the same period
Price has not moved more than 1.5x the breakout range beyond the level
exit_conditions: Close back inside the breakout level
Stop loss at 0.5 ATR above entry if mean reversion does not occur within 8 bars

Price has not extended more than 1.0x the breakout range beyond the level
exit_conditions: Close back inside the breakout level

rule_text: When significant price action is anticipated at a specific price level, price movement should occur at that level across all time frames; absence of action at that level invalidates the anticipated setup.
entry_conditions: Significant price level identified (support/resistance/key level)
Price action anticipated at that level across 3+ time frames (daily, 4H, 1H)
Actual price movement occurs within 1% of anticipated level on all observed time frames
Confirmation candles close at or beyond the level on smallest time frame
exit_conditions: Price fails to reach anticipated level on any confirmed time frame
Close beyond level without follow-through on 2 subsequent bars

rule_text: A trader should initiate trend-following positions on pullbacks following breakouts of significant levels, because strong order flow and market activity at the breakout level make flat consolidations unlikely; pullback failures typically occur through strong momentum or second tests rather than range compression.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above significant resistance level on high volume
Pullback retraces maximum 38% of initial breakout move
Price fails to penetrate pullback extreme on second test attempt
Momentum indicators show strengthening (RSI > 50 or MACD positive)
exit_conditions: Price closes below pullback extreme on strong volume
Breakout level is violated on close with volume confirmation

rule_text: A trader should enter a failed breakout trade when the market reverses sharply and fails out of the wrong side of the pullback formation, indicating loss of conviction outside the breakout level.
entry_conditions: Initial breakout occurs and holds outside pre-breakout range
Pullback forms within 33% of initial breakout move
Price reverses sharply and closes back THROUGH the pullback formation to the wrong side
Price then returns to and breaks back through the original breakout level (full retracement confirmation)
exit_conditions: Stop loss 1 ATR beyond the pullback extreme
Target at 50% of original breakout move in reverse direction

rule_text: A trader should enter a trade in the direction opposite to a failed breakout when sharp momentum emerges against the breakout direction, confirming that the breakout has failed.
entry_conditions: Price breaks below support level and consolidates below it
Sharp momentum bars (>1.5x average bar size) emerge AGAINST breakout direction
Price returns above the support level, closing above it for at least 1 bar
Volume on reversal momentum exceeds 150% of average volume
Wait for pullback to retrace 38-50% of the failed breakout move before entering
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1 ATR above the recent swing high of the failed breakout
Target: Previous consolidation high (before breakout attempt)
Time stop: Exit after 5 bars if no directional movement

exit_conditions: Exit entire position on first lower timeframe weakness during consolidation
Exit if consolidation breach is downward (below breakout entry)

rule_text: Enter a reversion trade (short) into a pullback when preceded by strong directional momentum in the opposite direction, especially when the pullback fails to reclaim a prior breakout level.
entry_conditions: Strong directional momentum (opposite to prior breakout) appears on primary timeframe
Pullback occurs with any failure to reclaim breakout level (A) by at least 0.25 ATR
Entry on any close below the pullback high OR on momentum divergence confirmation
exit_conditions: Stop loss: breakout level (A)
Take profit: trailing stop at 1.5 ATR or swing low
No time-based exit—hold for full trend development

rule_text: A trader should initiate long positions at pullback failures (point D) following breakout reversals, as these points offer high-probability entries in the direction of the original trend.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above level C (initial breakout attempt)
Price pulls back but closes above midpoint of C-to-D range
Price rejection occurs at or above 70% retracement of breakout move
Higher timeframe shows momentum continuation away from breakout level
exit_conditions: Close below level C
Lower high than point D fails to form on next attempt

rule_text: A trader should enter a position against the trend when momentum divergence appears alongside a failed pullback pattern, using specific risk management points to define the trade setup.
entry_conditions: Established trend in place
Pullback occurs into trend (retracement >= 38.2% of prior move)
Momentum indicator (RSI/MACD) fails to confirm new trend continuation (divergence: price higher, momentum lower or flat)
Price fails to extend beyond previous swing high/low (failed pullback confirmed)
Risk management level (stop loss point) identified at recent extreme or pattern boundary

parameters.momentum_divergence_threshold: Momentum indicator must be at least 1 standard deviation weaker than price action

exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at pre-identified risk management level
Trade moves 1.5x risk in profit; take partial or full profit
Price reverses back into trend and closes beyond entry level on higher timeframe close

rule_text: A trader should enter a position against the trend when momentum divergence appears alongside a failed pullback pattern, using specific risk management points to define the trade setup.
entry_conditions: Trend established (price higher highs/lows or lower highs/lows for 3+ candles)
Pullback into trend (retracement 30-70% of prior impulse)
Momentum divergence visible (RSI below 50 while price above 50, or MACD histogram smaller than prior bar)
Pullback fails to extend prior swing (lower high or higher low relative to trend direction)
Risk level defined at recent swing extreme or Fibonacci level

parameters.momentum_divergence_threshold: Momentum indicator weaker than price by at least 0.5 standard deviations
parameters.risk_stop_placement: At swing extreme within 1 ATR buffer

exit_conditions: Stop loss triggered
Trade reaches 2x initial risk; take profit partial position
Trend resumes beyond entry; hold with trailing stop

rule_text: A trader should enter a position against the trend when momentum divergence appears alongside a failed pullback pattern, using specific risk management points to define the trade setup.
entry_conditions: Trend present (2+ candles in direction)
Pullback begins (any retracement from recent swing)
Momentum indicator begins to diverge (RSI crosses below 50 in uptrend, or MACD histogram shrinks)
No requirement for pullback to fully fail; enter on divergence signal alone
Risk level set at recent swing or 2 ATR distance

parameters.momentum_divergence_threshold: Momentum indicator shows any visible weakness relative to prior bars
parameters.risk_stop_placement: At 2.0x ATR below/above entry or recent swing

exit_conditions: Stop loss at 2x ATR distance
Take profit at 1.5x risk (even if partial fill)
Exit if momentum re-confirms trend direction (RSI re-enters overbought/oversold)

rule_text: A trader should initiate counter-trend positions only after divergence appears AND is confirmed by lower timeframe price action, emerging market structure, extreme volatility, or multiple trend legs (3+), with higher conviction as trend legs accumulate.
entry_conditions: Divergence detected on primary timeframe
Lower timeframe price action confirms reversal structure
AND emerging market structure shows trend exhaustion
AND (trend_leg_count >= 4 OR volatility_percentile >= 85th)
exit_conditions: Original trend resumes (break of recent swing high)
Volatility mean-reverts below 50th percentile

rule_text: A trader should monitor pullback inflection points where price begins to reverse back toward the trend direction, and be prepared to exit or avoid entries if the pullback fails to resume the trend and instead enters a trading range consolidation.
entry_conditions: Price is in an established trend (min 3 bars higher highs or lower lows)
Pullback occurs (price retraces against trend)
Pullback inflection point identified (reversal bar or pattern off pullback low/high)
exit_conditions: Price fails to break beyond pullback start point within 2 bars of inflection
Price oscillates within defined range (high - low < 20 pips) for 2+ bars
Close stabilizes around mid-point of pullback zone

rule_text: A trader should monitor pullback inflection points where price begins to reverse back toward the trend direction, and be prepared to exit or avoid entries if the pullback fails to resume the trend and instead enters a trading range consolidation.
entry_conditions: Price is in an established trend (min 5 bars of directional movement)
Pullback occurs with clear reversal structure
Inflection point identified with candlestick confirmation (engulfing, pin bar, or reversal setup)
exit_conditions: Price fails to move beyond pullback entry level within 4 bars of inflection point
Price oscillates within defined range (high - low < 35 pips) for 4+ bars
Multiple failed attempts to break above/below range boundaries

rule_text: A trader should monitor pullback inflection points where price begins to reverse back toward the trend direction, and be prepared to exit or avoid entries if the pullback fails to resume the trend and instead enters a trading range consolidation.
entry_conditions: Price is in an established trend (min 8 bars of directional structure)
Pullback occurs (deeper retracements permitted)
Inflection point identified at support/resistance within pullback zone
exit_conditions: Price fails to move beyond pullback entry level within 6 bars of inflection
Price oscillates within defined range (high - low < 50 pips) for 6+ bars with at least 3 failed breakout attempts
Volume contracts during range formation confirming equilibrium

rule_text: A trader should enter a reversion trade when a pullback fails to penetrate the previous trend extreme, signaling exhaustion of countertrend momentum and equilibrium establishment before the original trend resumes.
entry_conditions: Original trend established (swing A confirms trend decisively ended)
Pullback fails to close beyond 95% of previous trend extreme
Strong countertrend thrust follows small pullback
Price exhausts near or slightly beyond MMO (within 100% of measured move)
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1 ATR above MMO + thrust high
Target: Measured move objective from swing A
Time stop: 20 bars without new trend extreme

Price fails to hold previous pivot extreme on second test

Volume or momentum divergence present on failed pullback
exit_conditions: Stop loss at swing high/low of failed pullback structure
Exit if price reclaims pivot extreme with strong conviction

rule_text: A trader should confirm trend termination by monitoring price action for failure patterns (springs/upthrusts) at trend extremes, and distinguish brief retest attempts from true trend continuation using momentum analysis beyond the previous extreme.
entry_conditions: Price has reached suspected trend extreme (swing high/low)
Failure pattern (spring or upthrust) has fully formed at the extreme
Momentum indicator (RSI or MACD) shows weakness/divergence at the extreme
No momentum surge beyond the previous extreme
exit_conditions: Strong momentum (RSI > 60 or MACD positive) breaks beyond prior extreme by 0.5% or more
Close beyond prior extreme with volume surge

Price closes beyond prior extreme on elevated volume

Exit at first target aligned with HTF trend direction
