rule_text: A trader should prepare to exit or reverse a trend position when parabolic range expansion and buying/selling climax behavior occurs, as these patterns contain inherent seeds of dramatic reversal despite their apparent strength.
entry_conditions: Price has extended into parabolic acceleration zone (>3 standard deviations from 20-period MA)
Volume spike exceeds 150% of 20-period average on climax bar
Close reverses (bullish candle closes below open) immediately after climax
exit_conditions: Exit entire position on close of reversal candle
Alternative: place stop 1 ATR above climax candle high

rule_text: A trader should identify trend changes by comparing the relative strength, duration, and magnitude of bounces in the original trend direction against legs moving in the new trend direction; reluctant, smaller bounces suggest a completed trend change.
entry_conditions: Price has completed a two-legged complex consolidation
Countertrend momentum leg is visibly sharper than pullback leg
Bounce in original trend direction is ≤40% of the magnitude of the countertrend leg
Time duration of bounce is ≤50% of the countertrend leg duration
Both price AND time conditions must be satisfied simultaneously
exit_conditions: Exit if bounce exceeds 45% of countertrend leg magnitude
Exit if bounce duration exceeds 55% of countertrend leg duration
Exit on close below prior consolidation low

rule_text: When a market completes two sharp opposing impulse moves followed by consolidation (triangle), expect a protracted consolidation period, particularly after parabolic climaxes.
entry_conditions: First sharp impulse move completed (magnitude > 2.5 ATR over N bars)
Countertrend move nearly equal in magnitude (magnitude > 2.0 ATR, reverses >80% of prior move)
Second move in original direction begins (lower high/higher low confirmed)
Triangle formation visible with tightening range
Pattern occurs following parabolic climax (5+ bar rally/selloff)
exit_conditions: Triangle breaks with breakout move exceeding 1.5 ATR
Time-based: 20 bars of consolidation without breakout (exit flat)
Stop: Move exceeds 3 ATR against consolidation thesis

rule_text: Avoid trading in consolidation zones following extended moves and recognized topping formations, as they represent low-probability, capital-inefficient setups that trap most traders.
entry_conditions: Price has completed a well-recognized topping pattern (H&S or double top)
Price enters consolidation phase (range-bound movement)
Consolidation duration exceeds 5 bars/periods
No new position initiated until price breaks consolidation range with volume
exit_conditions: Exit any existing long positions on topping pattern completion
Do not re-enter until consolidation is broken with price+volume confirmation

rule_text: A trader should probe and test the area beyond previous resistance highs to distinguish between trapped stops/fake-outs and genuine continuation buying pressure, as the initial move beyond resistance often attracts multiple buyer cohorts (stop-loss buyers, contrarian traders) whose commitment is ambiguous until tested.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above previous swing high
Initial momentum stalls and price pulls back toward the high
Price re-tests and holds above previous high on second attempt
Volume on second test is >= 80% of the initial breakout volume
exit_conditions: Price closes below previous high on retest
No volume confirmation on retest after 20 bars

rule_text: A trader should establish short positions or defensively exit long positions when price makes a failed breakout attempt (upthrust) after a consolidation period that suggested directional continuation, as this reversal pattern often precedes a sharp move in the opposite direction.

Confirmed close below second upthrust high on next bar
exit_conditions: Stop loss at highest point of second upthrust + 1 ATR
Take profit at lower consolidation boundary

rule_text: A trader should enter positions or increase conviction during major catalyst events when volatility spikes and feedback loops create large price movements, as these forced liquidations and repositioning create trading opportunities.
entry_conditions: Major news catalyst identified (company-specific, economic report, or commodity report)
Volatility (ATR or historical vol) increases by 50%+ from baseline
Price has already moved 2+ ATRs in one direction
Volume spike confirmed (>150% of 20-day average)
Wait 1-2 bars after initial shock for feedback loop confirmation
exit_conditions: Volatility contracts below 1.3x baseline for 3 consecutive bars
Stop loss at swing low/high prior to catalyst

rule_text: A trader should identify and act on a change of character in price movement—where the established trend pattern breaks and a new movement with strong counter-trend momentum emerges—by monitoring how price bars develop across timeframes, not relying on static end-of-day chart analysis alone.
entry_conditions: Established trend pattern is broken (price closes opposite to trend direction for N consecutive bars)
Lower timeframe (LTF) shows strong counter-trend momentum (RSI > 70 or < 30)
Higher timeframe (HTF) confirms character change (price crosses moving average)
Wait for at least 2 LTF bars of confirmed momentum before entry
exit_conditions: Exit if price reverses back through the broken pattern level
Exit on fixed profit target or HTF resistance/support

rule_text: A trader should restrict market activity exclusively to points where buying and selling pressure is imbalanced, as this imbalance represents the only exploitable edge in an otherwise highly efficient market.
entry_conditions: Imbalance magnitude exceeds 2.0 standard deviations from equilibrium
Imbalance persists for minimum 3 consecutive candles
Volume confirmation: imbalanced side shows >150% of average volume
exit_conditions: Exit when imbalance returns to within 1.0 stddev of equilibrium
Exit on close reversal below entry candle low/high

rule_text: A trader should avoid trend termination trades unless momentum divergence is confirmed by clear market structure changes, as trend terminations set up through momentum deterioration and are confirmed by shifts in character.
entry_conditions: Momentum divergence confirmed on primary timeframe
Lower timeframe shows reversal pattern structure (double-top, higher-low pattern, etc.)
Higher timeframe structure confirms deterioration (break of prior swing high/low in opposite direction)
Price has not yet moved more than 25% into the new direction
Volume profile shows distribution/accumulation shift
exit_conditions: Stop: New extreme beyond reversal point + 1ATR
Target: Prior swing level on higher timeframe

rule_text: Assess breakout trend strength by analyzing the first pullback after a breakout from a trading range; failure to hold above/below the breakout level indicates weakness.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance or below support of identified trading range
First pullback after breakout holds above (long) or below (short) the breakout level
Pullback does not recapture more than 50% of the distance from range to breakout point
exit_conditions: Price recaptures >50% of breakout move during pullback
Price closes back inside the original trading range

rule_text: Trade the underlying buying and selling pressure revealed by patterns, not the patterns themselves, by using precise entry/exit points and risk management tools that patterns define.
entry_conditions: Pattern structure identified with clear imbalance signal
Entry triggered only after price confirms pressure direction at pattern level
Volume confirms directional pressure
Entry positioned at pattern extremity (high probability zone)
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at 0.8x ATR from entry
Pattern structure broken (pressure reversal evident)
Time-based exit: 5-10 bars if pressure fails to develop

rule_text: Enter reversal trades after market probes fail at significant price levels, exploiting the quick reversal that occurs once stop orders are triggered, rather than entering during the initial probe itself.
entry_conditions: Market is overextended relative to short-term moving average (>2 std dev)
Price probes and closes beyond significant support/resistance level
Price reverses and closes back inside the level on next candle
Reversal candle has volume >1.5x average
Wait for third candle to confirm reversal direction
exit_conditions: Exit at 2x initial risk (1:2 reward/risk minimum)
Exit if price reverses back through entry within 3 candles

rule_text: Enter a short trade when price breaks above resistance in an overextended/primed-for-reversal market but immediately reverses and closes back below resistance on the same or next bar, trapping breakout traders.
entry_conditions: Price in mature extended uptrend OR extended consolidation below resistance
Momentum divergence present on trading timeframe
Price breaks above resistance level by minimum 0.5% of recent swing range
Price closes back below resistance on same or next bar
Close below resistance by minimum 0.3% of daily ATR
exit_conditions: Stop loss at resistance level + 0.5% buffer
Take profit at 50% of recent swing range below entry
Exit if price closes back above resistance

rule_text: A trader should use a partial profit-taking strategy scaled to initial risk, with heightened caution if the first profit target is not reached and consolidation occurs near the entry level, as this signals potential trend failure.
entry_conditions: Valid trend-following entry signal identified
Initial stop loss and risk amount defined
exit_conditions: Partial exit: when profit = initial risk on first tranche (typically 50% position)
Full exit: if price consolidates near entry within 50% of risk zone for 3+ bars without reaching first target

Setup shows potential for failure test reversal
exit_conditions: Exit only if price consolidates (range < 30 pips) persists beyond 3-4 bars of entry
Exit if price creates a consolidation pattern that extends beyond 4 bars near tested level
Exit on stop-loss hit

rule_text: A trader should enter trend-continuation trades by positioning during countertrend pullbacks at support (for longs) or resistance (for shorts) within an established larger trend, using a firm stop level and concrete trading plan.
entry_conditions: Primary trend established (price above/below 200-period MA)
Price retraces 50-61.8% of prior trend move
Support/resistance confirmed by multiple touches or volume
Entry at support (long) or resistance (short) with bounce confirmation candle
exit_conditions: Exit at recent trend high (long) or low (short)
Stop loss at support break (long) or resistance break (short)
Time stop: exit if no trend progress in 20 periods

Three-leg pattern formation begins (impulse-retracement-impulse initiation)
exit_conditions: Exit on reversal of momentum in setup leg

Exit on break of setup leg structure

Trend has completed at least 3 clean legs without false breaks
exit_conditions: Trend breaks below the prior leg's low
Timeframe drops below daily

rule_text: A trader should enter pullback trades in trending markets when price retraces with reduced bar ranges and absent countertrend momentum, as these conditions indicate valid pullbacks suitable for trend continuation participation.
entry_conditions: Primary uptrend or downtrend confirmed by higher highs/lows or moving average alignment
Price pulls back into support/resistance or moving average
Average bar range during pullback is ≤40% of pre-pullback average bar range
Volume during pullback is ≤50% of average preceding volume
No lower low (in uptrend) or higher high (in downtrend) by >0.5 ATR beyond pullback start
exit_conditions: Exit if price closes below pullback low (uptrend) or above pullback high (downtrend)
Exit if countertrend momentum exceeds 1.0 ATR
Exit on trend reversal signal

rule_text: Enter a long position when price penetrates below a support level (trend line or channel bottom) but recovers back above that support within a few bars, indicating weak sellers have been flushed out and strong buyers are reasserting control.
entry_conditions: Price penetrates below support level (trend line or channel bottom)
Price recovers and closes above support within 3-5 bars
Long shadow/wick below support visible (0.75-1.5% of bar range)
Entry on close or break above support once recovery confirmed
exit_conditions: Stop loss 0.75 ATR below the penetration low
Take profit at 1.5:1 risk-reward or next resistance

rule_text: Avoid buying pullbacks that test support three or more times, as multiple tests with lower highs indicate weakening buyer interest and higher probability of failure.
entry_conditions: Price is in an uptrend
Price has pulled back to support
Support level has been tested 2 or more times in the last 5-10 bars
High of the current bounce is lower than the previous bounce high
exit_conditions: Do not enter; wait for alternative setup

Stop loss: below the pivot point (C) if price reverses

rule_text: A trader should take partial or complete profits when price stalls near a profit target, even if the original target has not been reached, and should incorporate risk multiples as profit targets while also validating them against market structure and volatility-based levels.
entry_conditions: Pullback trade initiated with defined initial risk
Price approaches profit target area
exit_conditions: Take 50% of position at 0.5× risk multiple if price stalls 3+ bars
Take remaining 50% at 1.0× risk multiple or at conservative previous swing target
Require alignment with volatility-driven levels before holding for larger moves

rule_text: A trader should discriminate between strong trending markets and overextended (reversal-prone) markets before entering impulse-retracement-impulse pattern trades, as trades in overextended environments have significantly higher failure rates.
entry_conditions: Price is in retracement phase of impulse-retracement-impulse pattern
ADX > 25 (confirmed trend)
RSI(14) between 40-60 (not overbought/oversold)
Price has not made 2+ consecutive new highs within last 5 bars
Distance from 20-period high > 2% (not near exhaustion)
exit_conditions: ADX falls below 20 (trend weakening)
RSI crosses above 75 or below 25
Stop loss at 2% below retracement entry

rule_text: Enter trend continuation trades when price pulls back within an established trend, then breaks out in the direction of the primary trend on lower timeframes, combining pullback reliability with momentum confirmation.
entry_conditions: Price is in an established uptrend on the primary timeframe (e.g., 4H or daily)
Price retraces 50-61.8% of the most recent swing high-low
Support level holds at or near the 20-period moving average on primary timeframe
Lower timeframe (e.g., 15-min) breaks above the pullback high with volume confirmation
Momentum oscillator (RSI or MACD) on lower timeframe shows positive divergence or crosses above neutral zone
exit_conditions: Stop loss at pullback low (support breach)
Take profit at prior swing high
Trail stop above recent lower timeframe low once in profit

rule_text: Enter a pullback trade using a lower timeframe breakout trigger to confirm momentum alignment with the intended trade direction, accepting larger stops and lower reward/risk ratios in exchange for momentum confirmation.

Initial stop placed 1.0x the LTF breakout structure height below entry
exit_conditions: Exit on close below HTF pullback low
Exit on LTF breakdown below entry structure
Trail stop above recent LTF swing highs

rule_text: Use intraday timeframe breakouts as entry signals into higher timeframe patterns when the intraday momentum aligns with and provides context to a larger-timeframe trend or pullback structure.
entry_conditions: Intraday timeframe (5–15 min) breaks above recent resistance
Daily/weekly timeframe is in confirmed uptrend (price above 20-period MA)
Daily price is in pullback phase (below 5-day high, above 20-period MA)
Intraday breakout volume > 50th percentile of recent volume
exit_conditions: Close below intraday support (breakout swing low)
Daily close below 20-period MA
Time-based exit at end of trading day if position not in profit

rule_text: A trader should validate breakouts of the trading timeframe using lower timeframe price action before entry, rather than trading breakouts blindly, to achieve cleaner and higher-probability entries.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above/below prior bar high/low on trading timeframe
Lower timeframe (4x smaller) shows 2+ confluence signals: prior bar breakout + support/resistance level + swing point alignment
exit_conditions: Close below the entry bar low (long) or above entry bar high (short)
Stop loss at swing low/high prior to entry signal

rule_text: Enter a breakout through multiple bars with identical highs after a pullback setup, particularly effective intraday and in liquid markets, while accounting for gap risk on higher timeframes.
entry_conditions: 3 consecutive bars with identical high (within 1 tick)
Pullback retraces 38-50% of prior move
Breakout on close above the shared high
Timeframe: intraday only (≤ 1 hour)
exit_conditions: Stop loss: below the low of the pattern by 1 ATR
Target: 2:1 risk-reward minimum
Time stop: close position if no breakout within 5 bars

Pattern invalidation signal

rule_text: A trader should enter a breakout trade nested within a pullback pattern when the market structure shows a two-legged countertrend move, as the character of the move after the pullback reveals balance of buying and selling pressure.
entry_conditions: Established uptrend or downtrend confirmed by higher highs/lows over 20+ periods
First countertrend leg retraces 38.2% to 50% of prior leg
Second countertrend leg retraces 23.6% to 38.2% of first leg
Price breaks above (long) or below (short) the high/low of the second countertrend leg with volume confirmation
Close beyond breakout level confirms entry
exit_conditions: Hard stop at low (short) or high (long) of second pullback leg
Exit if price reverses with equal volume to entry breakout over 5 bars

Exit on reversal bar with above-average volume

rule_text: A trader should identify and trade complex pullback patterns (ABCD structures nested within higher timeframe trends) because they precede stronger, cleaner directional moves than simple pullbacks and must be recognized as distinct trading setups.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe trend is established (>2 swing highs/lows in direction)
Lower timeframe completes full ABCD pattern with A, B, C, D legs clearly defined
Retracement leg (C) does not exceed 78.6% of leg AB
Price enters leg D zone with momentum confirming higher timeframe direction
exit_conditions: Exit at invalidation of higher timeframe trend structure
Exit if lower timeframe ABCD structure fails to progress into leg D

rule_text: Avoid entering with-trend pullback trades immediately after parabolic expansions or climactic price moves, as these often mark trend exhaustion rather than continuation opportunities.
entry_conditions: Price is pulling back within an established trend
No parabolic expansion or climax pattern detected in prior 3-5 bars on base timeframe
Price has closed 2+ bars outside the climax range before entry
exit_conditions: Stop loss at prior swing low (pullback low)
Take profit at trend-line or prior swing high

No size increase based on consolidation type

rule_text: Enter a trend-reversal trade when the market exhibits a termination pattern followed by a distinct shift in momentum character opposite to the prevailing trend.

exit_conditions: Retracement of reversal move exceeds 50% within 5 periods
Original trend resumes with new momentum extreme in original direction

Level confirmed significant on both daily and weekly timeframes
Price has pulled back to level cleanly after initial formation
Level represents a major swing high/low from prior 6+ months
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 2x the range of the formation pattern
Take profit: 3x the breakout range
Exit on close beyond level if failed breakout occurs within 3 bars

Good breakout levels are usually levels that are clearly visible to all market partic-
ipants. For instance, there are levels that have been tested cleanly multiple times, and
rule_text: Enter trades when price breaks through a clearly visible, multi-tested resistance or support level, because the breakout will trigger additional volume and order flow from traders managing existing positions.
entry_conditions: Price has tested the level >= 4 times without penetration
All tests are within 1-2 ticks of each other (clean tests)
Level is obvious on daily or weekly chart (visible at multiple timeframes)
Volume increases on breakout candle >= 150% of 20-period average
Close beyond level by >= 2 ATR to confirm breakout
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1.5 ATR below entry
Take profit: 3:1 risk-reward ratio

rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades when price gaps through an important resistance level, even at unfavorable execution prices, because gap breakouts signal powerful shifts in market dynamics and often result in strong 'gap and go' moves that don't retrace.
entry_conditions: Price gaps above clearly defined resistance level
Gap size is at least 1.5% above resistance
No significant price action (failed tests) above the level before the gap
Enter on first pullback into the gap (not at gap open) to improve execution
Confirm breakout intent with volume or momentum indicator
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 50% of gap size below resistance level
Target at 3x gap size above resistance
Exit if price closes back below resistance on high volume

Volume confirmation at entry point
exit_conditions: Stop loss: Close below range bottom by 0.5 ATR
Profit target: Let winners run after breakout above range top

rule_text: Distinguish between temporary tactical price drops below support (normal breakout noise requiring re-entry) and structural breakdowns below support (indicating trend continuation), using price action consolidation as the differentiator.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above recent range/base
Initial entry on breakout above range high
exit_conditions: Exit only if market consolidates for 5+ bars below support level AND closes below support 3+ times
Otherwise, on first drop below support, hold with wide stop or re-enter on recovery

rule_text: Validate breakout entries by checking alignment with higher timeframe structure; a trading timeframe breakout gains additional confirmation and strength when it coincides with or aligns to a higher timeframe pullback or support level within an established trend.
entry_conditions: Clear breakout level established on trading timeframe
Breakout price is within bottom 33% of higher timeframe pullback range
Price has tested the breakout level and held it at least once
Higher timeframe is in an established uptrend or downtrend
exit_conditions: Close below the breakout level
Higher timeframe structure invalidated

FAILED BREAKOUTS
Trade Type
Support/resistanceholding.
Concept Mostbreakoutsfail.
Setup When mo
rule_text: A trader should prequalify breakout trades by focusing on patterns that tend to support successful breakouts, rather than trading all breakouts indiscriminately, because failed breakouts are more common than winning ones but can be offset by exceptional reward/risk ratios when entry conditions are selective.
entry_conditions: Price breaks support/resistance level
Breakout occurs with above-median volume confirmation
Prior consolidation pattern shows 3+ touches of the level
Breakout direction aligns with intermediate trend (20-50 period)
No divergence signals on momentum oscillator
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 1.5x breakout amplitude below entry
Take profit at 3.0x risk (high reward:risk ratio compensates for selectivity)
Exit on close below breakout level + 1 ATR

rule_text: Do not mechanically reverse a failed breakout position; instead, wait for confirmation that the breakout has genuinely failed before entering a counter-trend trade, as volatile breakouts with pullbacks that violate the level are common and lead to whipsaws.
entry_conditions: Breakout level violated by at least 1 close
Price closes below breakout level for 2+ consecutive bars
No short entry taken on initial violation
exit_conditions: Exit long at breakout level immediately (no hold)
Do not enter short position; wait for next setup

rule_text: A trader should exit or reduce a breakout trade when the first pullback after the breakout is disproportionately strong, as this signals probable breakout failure.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance or below support
Enter breakout position on close beyond support/resistance level
exit_conditions: Exit if first swing (down after up breakout, or up after down breakout) retraces >50% of breakout thrust
Measure thrust as: distance from breakout level to highest high (or lowest low) in first 5 bars post-breakout
If retracement exceeds 50%, close position immediately

rule_text: A trader should identify and trade failed pullbacks as a critical pattern confirmation, understanding the distinction between pullbacks that continue (resuming the original trend) versus those that fail (reversing the trend direction).

Volume or volatility contraction during pullback phase
exit_conditions: Stop loss: 1.5 ATR above pullback high
Take profit: Previous swing high + (breakout magnitude × 1)

rule_text: A trader should enter trades only within defined structural patterns that have a clear risk point, rather than trading in isolation based on price extremes or support/resistance levels alone.
entry_conditions: Overextended price move identified (e.g., RSI > 80 or < 20 on primary timeframe)
Pattern structure confirmed on primary timeframe (e.g., buying climax, reversal bar, failed test)
Higher timeframe (2+ levels up) confirms directional bias or support/resistance
Risk point is clearly defined at pattern extreme
Entry occurs after pattern closes or on first pullback confirmation
exit_conditions: Stop loss placed at pattern-defined risk point (e.g., climax high for short)
Exit on break of pattern structure support

rule_text: Use Antipattern formations and final failure tests to identify and trade trend transitions (markup-to-distribution and markdown-to-accumulation), while avoiding position entry in these zones until a breakout signal confirms the new regime.

Price closes beyond prior resistance/support by minimum 0.5% (ATR-adjusted)
Volume confirms breakout above 110% of average volume
No position held in current transition zone
exit_conditions: Price reverses 1.5x ATR from entry
Close below entry zone on daily timeframe
Failure test occurs without follow-through

rule_text: A trader should enter positions early in anticipation of a breakdown through support, using price action patterns and market structure transitions as signal posts to monitor regime health and trade the entire cycle.
entry_conditions: Price approaches identified support level
Support has been tested at least 2 times without breakdown
Price action shows reversal signals (pin bar, rejection candle) at support
Market structure shows lower highs or consolidation above support
exit_conditions: Support breaks decisively (close below support + 1 ATR)
Price recovers above prior swing high

exit_conditions: Tool fails to achieve win rate threshold over rolling 50-trade window
Tool becomes correlated >0.4 with another tool in use

rule_text: Use moving averages as a trend confirmation tool by scanning price structure relative to the moving average to quickly assess overall market direction and structure.
entry_conditions: Price is above MA by at least 2 ATR units (uptrend) OR below MA by at least 2 ATR units (downtrend)
MA slope is directional (price of MA[current] vs MA[20 bars ago] confirms direction)
Price has not touched MA in the last 10 bars (confirms established trend, not reversal zone)
exit_conditions: Price closes on opposite side of MA
Price penetrates MA by 1+ ATR in opposite direction

rule_text: A trader should initiate a trend-following trade when price consistently remains on one side of a moving average, and should confirm the trend direction by the slope of the moving average itself.
entry_conditions: Price closes above (uptrend) or below (downtrend) the MA for at least 80% of the last N bars
MA slope is positive (uptrend) or negative (downtrend)
MA slope magnitude > 0.5 pips per bar (or equivalent for instrument)
exit_conditions: Price closes on opposite side of MA
MA slope flattens below 0.2 pips per bar

rule_text: Avoid trading markets in equilibrium; focus trading activity only on markets exhibiting temporary imbalance between buying and selling pressure, identified by price oscillation around a moving average consensus level.
entry_conditions: Price has moved >2.0 standard deviations away from the moving average
Market has spent <40% of the last 20 bars within 1 ATR of the MA
ADX > 25 (confirming directional imbalance)
exit_conditions: Price returns within 0.5 ATR of moving average
ADX falls below 20
Market spends >50% of last 10 bars chopping around MA

rule_text: Avoid trading markets that oscillate close to intermediate-term moving averages, as proximity to the MA indicates absence of a tradable buying/selling imbalance and guarantees a challenging, low-edge trading environment.
entry_conditions: Calculate distance of current price from 20-period intermediate-term moving average
Reject entry if absolute distance < 0.5% of price
Require at least 5 of last 10 bars to close within the band to confirm choppy regime
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks > 2% beyond MA (indicating imbalance emerged)
Exit if fewer than 3 of last 10 bars close within the band (regime changed)

rule_text: Avoid initiating trades during overextended momentum moves; instead, wait for the market to return to short-term balance before entering pullback trades.
entry_conditions: Price has moved significantly away from moving average in a momentum phase
Price crosses back and closes within 0.5 ATR of the moving average
Volume is declining relative to momentum move
Momentum oscillator (RSI or MACD) shows divergence or mean-reversion signal
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks through moving average in opposite direction by 1 ATR
Exit if pullback fails to develop and momentum resumes beyond entry by 2 ATR

rule_text: Avoid initiating trades during overextended momentum moves; instead, wait for the market to return to short-term balance before entering pullback trades.
entry_conditions: Price makes a momentum move away from moving average of 2+ ATR
Price retraces 50-60% of that momentum move range
Moving average has not been penetrated (pullback has not reverted to mean)
Support/resistance level or Fibonacci level aligns with entry zone
exit_conditions: Exit if price closes through moving average by 0.75 ATR
Exit if momentum resumes and extends beyond entry by 1.5 ATR

rule_text: Avoid initiating trades during overextended momentum moves; instead, wait for the market to return to short-term balance before entering pullback trades.

Volume begins to decline relative to prior momentum bars
Price retraces just 25-35% of momentum move (partial pullback entry)
exit_conditions: Exit if momentum resumes immediately and extends beyond entry by 1 ATR
Exit if pullback does not develop and reverses back to entry level

rule_text: Use a moving average as a reference filter to prevent entering pullback trades when price is overextended (far away from the average), thereby avoiding trades in unsustainable market extensions.
entry_conditions: Price has pulled back from recent extreme
Absolute distance between price and moving average ≤ 0.5 × ATR(20)
Moving average slope is positive (uptrend) or negative (downtrend)
Price is not at a new extreme relative to last N bars
exit_conditions: Price reaches moving average
Price moves beyond 1.0 × ATR away from entry
Time-based: 5 bars without favorable movement

rule_text: A trader should use moving average slope as a trend confirmation signal, with the slope's timeframe aligned to the trend timeframe being analyzed, recognizing this works better as a discretionary scanning tool than as a standalone quantitative entry rule.
entry_conditions: Price is aligned with trend direction (above MA in uptrend, below in downtrend)
MA slope magnitude exceeds 0.015 (steep positive/negative slope)
MA period = 50-period for daily timeframe analysis
Slope maintained for minimum 3 consecutive bars
Use as confirmation filter only; do not trade slope crossovers alone
exit_conditions: MA slope reverses below 0.005
Price closes on wrong side of MA

Entry only when price touches outer channel boundary (emotional extreme)
exit_conditions: Price returns to moving average (consensus value)
Stop loss if price penetrates outer channel by 0.5x volatility

rule_text: A trader should calibrate volatility bands to contain 85-90% of trading activity range across market conditions to generate meaningful signals that avoid both insignificant noise and untouched extremes.
entry_conditions: Price bar engages (touches or closes beyond) the upper or lower Keltner band
Confirm that bands contain 90% of all trading activity across lookback period
Bar must close outside the band, not merely touch it
exit_conditions: Price returns inside both bands for 2+ consecutive bars
Stop loss at opposite band

rule_text: A trader should fade (reverse) moves that extend beyond established channel boundaries, as there is a statistical tendency for price to revert back through those boundaries.

Price has extended 1.0x (ATR or channel width) beyond the band
Volume is present but not extreme
Wait for a reversal candlestick pattern (engulfing, pin bar) before entry
exit_conditions: Price closes back inside the Keltner channel
Initial stop loss at 1.5x extension distance beyond entry
Time-based exit after 5 bars without mean reversion confirmation

rule_text: A trader should only initiate fade (mean reversion) trades when price has moved beyond the volatility bands, indicating true market extension rather than equilibrium, combined with strict risk management.
entry_conditions: Price closes beyond outer Bollinger Band or Keltner Channel
Price has extended 2+ standard deviations from moving average
RSI or similar momentum indicator confirms extension (>70 or <30)
Volume confirms the move is genuine extension, not noise
exit_conditions: Price returns to moving average (mean)
Stop loss hit at 1.5% of entry
Time-based exit after 5 bars if mean not reached

rule_text: A trader should enter trades by either fading price moves that close outside volatility bands or entering pullbacks that touch the moving average after the band has been breached, capitalizing on mean-reversion behavior within defined price structure.
entry_conditions: Price closes outside Keltner Channel band (not mere touch)
OR: Price touches/crosses MA after band was previously breached, with close confirmation
Fade: Short when close above upper band; Long when close below lower band
Pullback: Long at MA after upper band touch; Short at MA after lower band touch

parameters.entry_trigger_pullback: Touch of band + return to MA with close confirmation

exit_conditions: Fade: Exit on close back inside band
Pullback: Exit on re-touch of opposite band or at 2xATR profit target

Pullback: Exit on re-touch of band or 1.5xATR profit

rule_text: A trader should enter trades by either fading price moves that close outside volatility bands or entering pullbacks that touch the moving average after the band has been breached, capitalizing on mean-reversion behavior within defined price structure.
entry_conditions: Price touches (not necessarily closes outside) Keltner Channel band

Fade: Short on upper band touch; Long on lower band touch (any part of candle)
Pullback: Enter at MA itself or within 1 ATR, any touch counts as entry signal

parameters.entry_trigger_fade: Touch of band (no close confirmation needed)

exit_conditions: Fade: Exit on first touch of MA or 1 ATR profit
Pullback: Exit on band re-touch or 1 ATR profit

rule_text: A trader should distinguish between normal market cycles (impulse-consolidation alternation with pullbacks at extremes) and distorted cycles (where extreme strength or weakness short-circuits normal mean-reversion), and trade accordingly.
entry_conditions: Price has moved to band extremes (1.5σ to 2.0σ from mean)
Pullback into middle band zone (±0.5σ from mean) confirmed
No price acceleration or extreme volatility spike detected
Previous impulse move is <2 standard deviations
exit_conditions: Exit if price reverses back to opposite band extreme
Exit if volatility spikes above 75th percentile
Profit target at middle band ±0.25σ

rule_text: When price presses into and remains at an extreme band boundary (upper or lower) with no pullback, this indicates exhaustion and one-sided imbalance; professionals should exit longs (or avoid entries) and stand aside rather than chase pullbacks.
entry_conditions: Price sustains within upper or lower band for >= 8 consecutive bars
Pullback depth < 20% of prior move amplitude
No close back to midline or beyond in lookback period
exit_conditions: Close position immediately upon 8-bar band duration + low pullback confirmation
Stand aside until price recaptures midline with normal pullback behavior

Re-enter only after pullback structure returns (pullback of minimum 3 bars showing lower highs)

rule_text: A trader should avoid trading and wait for consolidation when a market makes a sharp move in one direction immediately followed by an equally sharp reversal through the opposite band, as this environment typically leads to triangle consolidation and reduced directional clarity.
entry_conditions: Price spikes above upper Bollinger Band by >1.5 standard deviations
Price immediately reverses and spikes below lower Bollinger Band by >1.5 standard deviations within 3 bars
Consolidation pattern (ATR < 50th percentile of 20-bar ATR) is confirmed over 5+ bars before re-entry
exit_conditions: Do not enter until triangle consolidation is fully formed and volatility contracts below median
If early entry, exit on first move back through either band

Let’s turn our attention to some practical applications of the MACD, and how it might

rule_text: Use MACD to identify momentum inflection points and exhaustion zones where price momentum is likely to reverse or fail to continue after consolidation.

exit_conditions: Exit on momentum reversal signal reversal (MACD turns back toward exhaustion direction)
Exit on fixed profit target of 1.5x risk
Exit on time stop of 20 bars

rule_text: Use percentile-based reference levels derived from recent indicator history to identify extreme conditions for mean-reversion entries, rather than fixed absolute thresholds.
entry_conditions: Indicator value > 60th percentile of lookback window OR < 40th percentile
Any observable excursion away from center
Immediate entry on directional reversal signal
exit_conditions: Price returns to 50th percentile or crosses through entry level
Tight stop loss at percentile 55 or 45

Do NOT enter trend continuation trades
exit_conditions: N/A - do not enter position

rule_text: A trader should clearly define and selectively use momentum divergences within a predetermined trading plan that respects the current trend regime, rather than using them mechanically as universal trade signals or exit triggers.
entry_conditions: Momentum divergence detected
Price NOT in strong trend (ADX < 30 or equivalent)
Divergence confirmed by price action structure (failed breakout or rejection level)
Trade only if it aligns WITH minor trend direction
exit_conditions: Exit on break of divergence anchor point
Exit if trend resumes strength
Max hold: 2 bars after entry

rule_text: A trader should use higher timeframe structure to filter and add confidence to lower timeframe trades, and use lower timeframe price action to identify precise entries and validate whether higher timeframe support/resistance will hold.

exit_conditions: Exit on violation of higher timeframe key level
Exit on lower timeframe failed pattern

rule_text: A trader should align lower timeframe entries with higher timeframe pattern formations (climax moves, pullback-to-momentum transitions, range extremes with accumulation/distribution signs) because lower timeframe price action will be molded toward resolving the higher timeframe pattern.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe shows at least 2 of: climax move completion, pullback emerging into new momentum, or range extreme with volume confirmation
Lower timeframe shows early-stage structure aligned with higher timeframe direction
Clear accumulation or distribution signature visible on higher timeframe
exit_conditions: Higher timeframe pattern fails to resolve as expected
Lower timeframe structure breaks alignment with higher timeframe intent

rule_text: Use well-calibrated bands or channels (like Keltner channels) to identify overextension and distinguish between moves likely to reverse versus moves with continuation potential, rather than making standard practice of trading in the direction of strength on a single timeframe.
entry_conditions: Price touches outer band of Keltner channel (>2.0 ATR from midline)
Pullback begins toward midline
Entry triggered on retest or reversal confirmation
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks through midline without reversing
Exit if new extreme is made in original direction

rule_text: A trader should avoid entries on pullbacks in lower timeframes when the higher timeframe shows overextension and exhaustion signals, as these trades are prone to immediate failure.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe shows pullback consolidation after strong move
Lower timeframe shows return of directional momentum
Higher timeframe does NOT show tail beyond channel bands
Higher timeframe does NOT show exhaustion candle pattern
exit_conditions: Entry rejected if higher timeframe tail extends >2.5 standard deviations beyond bands
Entry rejected if higher timeframe shows 2+ consecutive exhaustion candles

rule_text: A trader should reject a trade setup on a lower timeframe when the higher timeframe chart provides contradictory evidence or fails to support the directional bias.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe (daily) shows entry signal (trend continuation, divergence reversal, or pattern completion)
Weekly chart shows clear directional support (higher highs/lows for long; lower highs/lows for short)
Weekly chart momentum indicator (MACD) is in agreement with direction (line above zero for long; below for short)
No exhaustion patterns (e.g., multiple pushes against resistance with divergence) on weekly chart
exit_conditions: Exit if weekly chart develops contradictory pattern during holding period
Exit if higher timeframe momentum divergence emerges

rule_text: Use multiple timeframe analysis to filter trade direction: when lower timeframes show strong consolidation patterns aligned with trend, use higher timeframe divergence signals as confirmation to enter counter-trend trades with reduced risk.
entry_conditions: Primary trend is strong uptrend (higher highs, higher lows on weekly)
Daily chart shows high-tight consolidation (narrow range, 5-10 bars minimum)
MACD shows negative divergence on BOTH weekly AND daily (lower highs in MACD despite higher price highs)
Price extends beyond upper Bollinger Band on weekly chart
Entry triggered only after daily consolidation breakout downward
exit_conditions: Exit if price closes back above consolidation high on daily
Exit if MACD shows positive divergence (low on daily higher than prior low)
Trail stop at recent swing high + 1 ATR

rule_text: When price extends beyond normal volatility bands on a lower timeframe, consult a higher timeframe to assess whether the move is a localized overextension or part of a broader systemic shock; adjust trade conviction and risk parameters accordingly.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe (5-min): Price overextends past Bollinger Band
Higher timeframe (30-min): Price within or slightly beyond 1.5 standard deviations of Bollinger Band
No evidence of exogenous shock (news, gap, volatility spike > 2 standard deviations)
exit_conditions: Price mean-reverts to 20-SMA on lower timeframe
Higher timeframe shows continued extreme overextension (trade aborted pre-entry)

rule_text: A trader should initiate continuation trades on the lower timeframe when a pullback occurs on a higher timeframe that exhibits high-probability pullback characteristics, because higher timeframe pullback confluence adds confidence and motivation to lower timeframe entries.
entry_conditions: HTF shows good impulse setting up continuation
HTF market is not overextended (RSI < 70 on HTF)
HTF is on first or second trend leg only (not third+)
HTF shows no momentum divergence (LTF and HTF momentum aligned)
HTF shows no buying or selling climax (volume not spiking)
HTF pullback volume lower than prior trend leg volume
LTF pullback initiates within HTF pullback zone
exit_conditions: Exit at HTF pullback high if price breaks structure without new ATR-based higher low
Exit at 2:1 risk-reward minimum on LTF

rule_text: A trader should increase conviction and enter a trade setup when a lower timeframe pattern receives confirmation from alignment with a higher timeframe pattern in the same direction.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe (daily) shows clean pullback or consolidation pattern
Higher timeframe (weekly) shows completed impulse move followed by first reaction bounce/pullback
Both timeframes directionally aligned (both bullish or both bearish context)
Entry only on breakdown/breakdown of lower timeframe pattern after weekly confirmation
exit_conditions: Stop loss at impulse swing extreme on weekly chart
Take profit at next weekly resistance/support level

rule_text: Use higher timeframe chart patterns to establish bullish or bearish context that justifies aggressive pursuit of aligned setups on lower timeframes, even when lower timeframe price action appears choppy or unconvincing.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe shows marginal new extreme (low or high) with RSI/momentum divergence
Higher timeframe reversal bar closes beyond the extreme with momentum confirmation
2+ bars have passed confirming the higher timeframe reversal (minimum confirmation window)
Lower timeframe shows at least one complete impulse move (3+ bars) aligned with higher timeframe direction
Lower timeframe momentum is not severely overbought/oversold in entry direction

exit_conditions: Exit if higher timeframe reversal is broken on close
Exit on 2x ATR stop below/above entry based on direction

rule_text: A trader should reject or skip trades that align with a smaller timeframe pattern when a higher timeframe chart shows a conflicting bias or incomplete consolidation pattern.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe shows consolidation breakout signal (e.g., daily chart break above resistance)
Higher timeframe (weekly or monthly) shows unresolved or ambiguous consolidation pattern
Higher timeframe bias is opposite to lower timeframe directional signal
exit_conditions: Do not enter; wait for higher timeframe consolidation to resolve with matching bias

rule_text: A trader should use multi-timeframe confirmation to filter trade signals—specifically, avoiding or downsizing trades where lower timeframe patterns contradict the bias of higher timeframe patterns, especially at inflection points.
entry_conditions: Lower timeframe pattern generates signal (e.g., daily breakout)
Higher timeframe pattern (weekly/monthly) confirms signal direction
No contradictory pattern structure visible on higher timeframe
Entry is NOT at a recognized inflection point on higher timeframe
exit_conditions: Exit immediately if higher timeframe pattern fails during hold
Exit on stop loss if lower timeframe reverses

Both timeframes aligned in direction

Exit at higher timeframe target or 2R profit

rule_text: A trader should use lower timeframe price action to refine exits from trades established on higher timeframes, but must balance this against overreaction to insignificant noise.
entry_conditions: Position already established from higher timeframe setup
Lower timeframe shows reversal structure (e.g., break of lower timeframe swing high/low with confirmation candle)
Volume or momentum confirmation present on lower timeframe
exit_conditions: Exit only when lower timeframe shows two consecutive reversal bars below/above swing structure
AND lower timeframe move exceeds 1.5x ATR of that timeframe

rule_text: Trade reversions from higher timeframe consolidation range boundaries (support/resistance) when price tests beyond the range and immediately reverses, because such Wyckoff-pattern reversals are cleaner, more tradable setups than random price action within ranges.
entry_conditions: Price breaks beyond established HTF consolidation range boundary (support or resistance)
Price has closed back inside the range on the HTF
Lower timeframe shows 2+ consecutive reversionary candles against the breakout direction
Entry is placed at the range boundary, not chasing the reversal
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at HTF range boundary + 1 ATR
Take profit at midpoint of HTF consolidation range

rule_text: Scan multiple timeframes to identify trending opportunities within higher-timeframe consolidation zones, using lower timeframes for precise entry timing.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe (e.g., weekly) is in established trading range with defined support/resistance
Middle timeframe (e.g., daily) shows trend initiation off tested resistance/support
Lower timeframe (e.g., 4-hour) confirms directional momentum aligned with middle timeframe trend
Price action on lower timeframe shows clean break of pivot level with volume confirmation
exit_conditions: Break of highest timeframe consolidation range
Lower timeframe trend reversal with close below entry support
Stop loss at most recent swing low before entry signal

rule_text: A trader should use higher timeframes to filter which trades are likely to succeed and lower timeframes to time precise entries, creating a multi-timeframe confirmation structure.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe (HTF) shows established trend or clear directional bias
Intermediate timeframe (MTF) shows price action consistent with HTF direction
Entry timeframe (LTF) shows precise entry pattern (support bounce, breakout, or reversal) aligned with HTF/MTF direction
All three timeframes in agreement on direction
exit_conditions: Price violates support/resistance on entry timeframe
Higher timeframe structure breaks

rule_text: Use lower timeframe price action to time entries into structural support/resistance levels and pivots identified on higher timeframes, rather than entering on the higher timeframe signal alone.

exit_conditions: Invalidation: price closes back through the structural level on HTF
Trailing stop 1.5 ATR below entry on LTF

Lower timeframe shows any directional bar or break of the prior LTF bar in direction away from HTF structure
Entry triggered on LTF bar close moving away from structure
exit_conditions: Invalidation: HTF closes back through structure or creates structure contradiction
Fixed stop 0.5 ATR below entry or below prior LTF bar low

rule_text: A trader should enter a breakout trade when lower timeframe momentum aligns with and breaks through a structure that is already at a tipping point on a higher timeframe, because the confluence amplifies the predictable move disproportionately.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe (e.g., daily/weekly) shows price at key resistance/support within 1-2% of identified tipping point
Lower timeframe (e.g., 1h/4h) produces a breakout above/below recent equilibrium level with volume > 1.5x average
Both timeframes break in same direction simultaneously or within 1-2 candles
Structure on higher timeframe already showing stress (multiple tests, narrowing range)
exit_conditions: Close below/above breakout level (reverse the direction of entry)
Stop loss at 2x the size of the lower timeframe breakout move

rule_text: A trader should use relative strength across multiple timeframes and related markets as a confidence filter for trade entries and justification for early exits from losing positions.
entry_conditions: Primary timeframe (daily) shows directional setup
Relative strength confirmation in 2 higher timeframes (weekly, monthly)
Related asset/market (same sector/correlated pair) shows relative outperformance in same direction
At least 3 of 4 strength confirmations aligned
exit_conditions: Relative strength divergence appears (price makes new high but strength does not)
Loss of relative outperformance in related markets
Strength confirmation drops below 3 aligned conditions

Another instrument in group shows significantly stronger momentum (>10% relative strength lead)

rule_text: Follow institutional money flows by trading relative strength leaders (long the strongest markets with institutional support) and avoid mean-reversion bargain hunting; manage the tension between momentum and reversion through disciplined rebalancing.

Position sizing capped at 2% risk per trade

Exit on mean reversion signal (price drops below 10-day MA)

rule_text: When trading relative strength, avoid entering at peaks of overbought conditions by incorporating mean reversion and range expansion filters alongside strength rankings to improve entry quality.
entry_conditions: Relative strength rank > 60th percentile
Price within 40–70th percentile of recent range (early pullback, not extreme)
Either: mean reversion underway (price moved 0.5–1.0 ATR from extreme) OR volatility rising (>1.2x average)
Confirmation bar closes above entry signal level
exit_conditions: Exit if relative strength rank falls below 45th percentile
Exit if price reverts below 30th percentile of range

rule_text: When trading relative strength, avoid entering at peaks of overbought conditions by incorporating mean reversion and range expansion filters alongside strength rankings to improve entry quality.

Enter on close above setup bar
exit_conditions: Exit if relative strength rank falls below 40th percentile
Exit on 2x ATR stop loss from entry

rule_text: Buy the strongest relative strength candidates on weakness using technical patterns as triggers, then monitor for resumption of leadership; exit or adjust if leadership does not resume.

Candidate is in top 25th percentile of relative strength over lookback period

Pattern completion confirmed (close above pattern level or second touch of support)
Volume confirmation of pattern (volume on reversal bar >= 120% of 20-bar average)
exit_conditions: Exit if price fails to close above pattern high within 5 bars of entry
Exit if price breaks below pattern low on increased volume
Exit if relative strength rank drops below 50th percentile
Trail stop at recent swing low + 1 ATR

rule_text: Buy the strongest relative strength candidates on weakness using technical patterns as triggers, then monitor for resumption of leadership; exit or adjust if leadership does not resume.
entry_conditions: Market trend bias established
Candidate is in top 50th percentile (or top 3 in peer group) by relative strength
Price shows any pullback from recent swing high (minimum 1-2 bars down)
Early reversal signal triggered: hammer, pin bar, inside bar breakout, or first higher close after down bar
Entry on close of reversal bar or first bar above recent swing low
exit_conditions: Strict 2-bar stop: exit if price makes new swing low + closes below entry
Exit if relative strength deteriorates sharply (drops 20+ percentiles within 2 bars)
Trail very tight: 0.75 ATR below entry, ratchet up daily

rule_text: Buy pullbacks into trending strength when momentum does not develop extreme countertrend readings; exit or adjust if weakness persists too long and leaders lose their leadership position.
entry_conditions: Price in established uptrend (higher highs, higher lows)
Price pulls back to support level
MACD momentum does not make new extreme low vs recent pullback lows (within 20% of prior pullback low)
Relative strength measure confirms leader still in control
exit_conditions: Exit if MACD makes new extreme countertrend low
Exit if price fails to make new high within 10 bars
Exit if pullback exceeds 23.6% of prior move

rule_text: Maintain exposure to relative strength leaders based on their trend integrity and trading patterns, not just their current ranking; avoid abandoning leaders on minor ranking fluctuations within the peer group.
entry_conditions: Instrument ranks in top 20% of sector/asset class by relative strength
Price maintains higher lows and higher highs (unbroken uptrend)
No recent trend break or reversal pattern on primary timeframe
exit_conditions: Relative strength ranking drops below top 25%
Price closes below most recent swing low
Trend integrity breaks (lower high or lower low)

rule_text: Use relative strength analysis as a screening mechanism to identify when genuine buying and selling imbalances exist before entering trades, rather than relying on simplistic technical patterns taken out of context.
entry_conditions: Relative strength of target instrument > 65th percentile vs peer group (20-day lookback)
Relative strength of target sector > 60th percentile vs broader market (20-day lookback)
Technical pattern present AND confirmed by RS signal on both daily and weekly timeframes
Institutional buying pressure evident in volume/price structure
exit_conditions: Relative strength falls below 50th percentile
Sector relative strength reverses below 55th percentile

rule_text: A trader must consciously calibrate exit discipline (profit-taking and stop-loss sizing) to their own personality and risk tolerance, because exit parameter choices directly impact expectancy and edge preservation even when the underlying pattern has statistical edge.
entry_conditions: Edge-based pattern identified in technical setup
exit_conditions: Exit at small predetermined profit (1:1 ratio) OR small predetermined loss (1:1 ratio)
Enforce hard stops to prevent expectancy erosion

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch. P1:OTA, pp. 247–278
raw_chunk_text:
PLACING THE INITIAL STOP
The placement of the initial stop is very important. First, it sets the ultimate risk point
on the trade and so removes much of the emotion from the trade process. This point
rule_text: A trader should place initial stops at a distance that reflects the trade-off between position size and win probability, using the stop distance to inform position sizing that removes emotion from trade management.
entry_conditions: Technical setup identified with clear, nearby support/resistance
Stop distance ≤ 0.5% of instrument price (tight stop)
exit_conditions: Price hits initial stop loss
Position reaches 2:1 or 3:1 reward-to-risk

TradeManagement 233
Fixed-Percentage Stops
Old-schoolstocktraders,inparticular,tendtodothis:simplygetintoapositionandgive
it1
rule_text: A trader should calibrate fixed stop-loss percentages to the volatility characteristics of each market rather than applying a uniform percentage across all markets.
entry_conditions: Position is established
Calculate average daily range (ADR) over last 20 trading days
ADR = average of (daily_high - daily_low) / daily_close
exit_conditions: Exit long position if price falls below entry_price - (ADR × 1.5)
Exit short position if price rises above entry_price + (ADR × 1.5)

Old-schoolstocktraders,inparticular,tendtodothis:simplygetintoapositionandgive
it1
rule_text: A trader should calibrate fixed stop-loss percentages to the volatility characteristics of each market rather than applying a uniform percentage across all markets.
entry_conditions: Position is established
Calculate average daily range (ADR) over last 20 trading days
ADR = average of (daily_high - daily_low) / daily_close
exit_conditions: Exit long position if price falls below entry_price - (ADR × 2.0)
Exit short position if price rises above entry_price + (ADR × 2.0)

rule_text: A trader should calibrate fixed stop-loss percentages to the volatility characteristics of each market rather than applying a uniform percentage across all markets.
entry_conditions: Position is established
Calculate average daily range (ADR) over last 20 trading days
ADR = average of (daily_high - daily_low) / daily_close

rule_text: A trader should calibrate stop-loss distance to the market's volatility rather than using a fixed percentage, because fixed stops may fall within normal price noise and fail to represent intelligent risk management.
entry_conditions: Trade is entered based on primary strategy signal
Calculate market ADR over last 20 trading days
Confirm ADR is not at extreme historical levels
exit_conditions: Price moves beyond 2.5 × ADR below entry point (long trade)
Price moves beyond 2.5 × ADR above entry point (short trade)

rule_text: A trader should set stop-loss placement based on market structure—specifically by identifying and respecting pivot points and support/resistance levels—rather than using fixed distance or volatility-only methods.
entry_conditions: Short-term momentum signal confirmed
Identify nearest pivot point (daily swing high/low, support/resistance level)
Position entry at favorable price relative to structure
exit_conditions: Stop hit if price closes beyond 1.5× pivot distance
Take profit at next structural resistance

rule_text: A trader should adjust position size downward when facing elevated uncertainty (impending economic events, volatility spikes) that could generate excessive price movement and stop losses.
entry_conditions: Support/resistance level identified on chart
Economic calendar event expected within next 4 trading sessions OR VIX > 20 OR ATR expansion > 30% from 20-period mean
exit_conditions: Stop loss at most extreme high/low beyond current support/resistance
Exit at initial profit target OR if stop is hit on fakeout, re-enter with new stop beyond new extreme

rule_text: A trader must define a precise initial risk point (stop loss) at trade entry and respect it without exception, adjusting position size inversely when volatility is elevated to manage overall portfolio risk.
entry_conditions: Trade setup identified with entry price defined
Volatility regime assessed (use ATR or IV percentile)
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at initial risk point (no exception)
Position size = base_size × reduction_factor based on current volatility

SETTING PRICE TARGETS
To set or not to set price targets, that is the question. Many traders prefer to not set
pricetargetsinanefforttofollowthemaxim“letyourwinnersrun.”Othertradersmake
thecogentargum
rule_text: A trader should choose between fixed profit targets with partial exits or open-ended position management, potentially combining both approaches by taking partial profits at preset levels while allowing remainder to run for larger moves.
entry_conditions: Trade enters with positive expectancy signal
Position size determined by fixed stop-loss distance
exit_conditions: Exit entire position when price reaches fixed profit target
Exit entire position if stop-loss touched

Manual cancellation if regime changes

rule_text: A trader should scale out of winning positions at predefined profit multiples of initial risk, reducing position size progressively while letting remainder run under separate rules.

Position established with defined initial risk (R)

Exit 50% of remaining when profit = 5.0× initial risk
Manage final 16.7% with independent risk rules

rule_text: Align entry order placement with market structure (pivot highs/lows) to improve risk-reward by tightening orders inside visible swing points rather than beyond them.

Calculate intended entry based on risk multiple target
If calculated entry is beyond pivot point, move order inward
exit_conditions: Exit at profit target adjusted downward by the inset amount
Stop loss remains at structural level

rule_text: Avoid using simple trend line breaks as stop levels; instead require significant price action (multiple closes, consolidation, or multiple legs) below the trend line before treating it as a trailing stop signal, as premature stops are often reversed and miss subsequent trend continuation.
entry_conditions: Position established with trend line defined
Price closes below trend line
Price closes below trend line for 2 additional consecutive bars (3 closes total)
exit_conditions: Exit trailing stop when 3 consecutive closes occur below trend line

rule_text: Avoid using simple trend line breaks as stop levels; instead require significant price action (multiple closes, consolidation, or multiple legs) below the trend line before treating it as a trailing stop signal, as premature stops are often reversed and miss subsequent trend continuation.

Price breaks below trend line creating an initial impulse leg
Price retraces partway back (minimum 38% of impulse leg)
Price breaks below trend line again, creating a second impulse leg
exit_conditions: Exit when second impulse leg below trend line is confirmed with qualifying retracement between legs

rule_text: A trader should place and dynamically tighten a protective stop loss by moving it incrementally closer to an extreme trend point using an acceleration factor that increases over time, thereby progressively locking in gains as the trend extends.
entry_conditions: Identify most recent extreme point (highest high in uptrend or lowest low in downtrend)
Confirm trend is active and intact
exit_conditions: Stop loss is hit
Trend reversal confirmed (new extreme in opposite direction)

rule_text: Use the Parabolic SAR as a dynamic trailing stop mechanism in trending markets, adjusting the acceleration factor to balance responsiveness to reversals against whipsaw avoidance in choppy price action.
entry_conditions: Position already initiated in direction of established trend
Price has made at least 2 confirmed higher highs (uptrend) or lower lows (downtrend)
ATR-based volatility is below 50th percentile for 20-period lookback
exit_conditions: Parabolic SAR flips and closes beyond SAR level (trend reversal signal)
Stop loss triggered at SAR level
Take partial profit at first SAR-defined pivot before moving stop to breakeven

Suitable for scalping or very short-term trend riding; re-enter on next signal

Position entered near trend start

No acceleration tightening applied

rule_text: Avoid stop-loss placement at statistical turning points where mean reversion is likely; subject all stop methodologies to statistical testing and multi-trade backtesting rather than cherry-picking ideal trending environments.
entry_conditions: Long entry established
Verify stop placement is NOT at 2+ consecutive closes opposite to trade direction
Backtest sample size ≥ 500 trades across trending AND ranging AND high-volatility regimes
exit_conditions: Exit at predetermined stop level
Stop must not be placed within zone of likely mean reversion (first 2 closes against position)

rule_text: A trader should use automated or fixed systems to remove emotional decision-making during real-time position management, especially at market extremes where emotional panic (capitulation at lows, desperation covering at highs) drives price.

Position size predetermined and non-negotiable

No mid-trade management or averaging

rule_text: A trader should pre-define both an absolute dollar risk amount and an ultimate drop-dead stop level, then continuously track position size and average price to ensure the loss at that stop level never exceeds the pre-defined risk amount.
entry_conditions: Define absolute dollar risk amount = 0.5% of account
Set ultimate stop loss distance = 1.5x ATR from entry
Calculate maximum position size = risk_amount / stop_distance
Do not scale in; maintain fixed position size
exit_conditions: Exit at pre-defined ultimate stop loss level without exception
Do not move stop closer to entry after partial profits

rule_text: A trader should scale into extended trending positions by reducing position size at range extremes to preserve dry powder and capital for continuation moves, accepting smaller wins in ranging periods in exchange for outsized profits during multi-leg trends.
entry_conditions: Market identified in multi-leg uptrend or downtrend
Price at 75th+ percentile of recent range (top) or 25th percentile (bottom)
Position size reduced to 40% of maximum allocation at range extremes
Build remaining 60% across subsequent trend legs away from range edges
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at first profit target after range extreme
Trail stops on remaining position by 2 ATR
Exit on first reversal signal (close below/above key level)

rule_text: When scaling into a position during volatile market moves with evidence of expanding volatility, a trader may widen the stop loss on the initial partial position rather than completing the full intended position size, allowing for larger adverse moves while preserving capital for scaled entry at potentially better prices.
entry_conditions: Scaling into a directional move (partial position established)
Volatility metric (ATR, historical vol) expands by >40% from entry
Expansion confirmed by external catalyst (news event, range breakout)
Current position size <50% of intended final size
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at widened level
Volatility normalizes back to ±15% of pre-expansion levels, then resume original plan
Time stop: resume original scaling after 5 bars if no further expansion

rule_text: A trader should reduce or hedge correlated positions during periods of financial stress or crisis, because correlations between diverse assets converge toward 1.0 in such regimes, eliminating the diversification benefit.
entry_conditions: VIX > 20 OR credit spread widening > 50bps
Calculate rolling 30-day correlation between primary position and portfolio holdings
Trigger hedge when correlation exceeds 0.70
exit_conditions: Hedge removed when VIX < 15 AND credit spreads normalize AND rolling correlation falls below 0.60 for 10 consecutive days

rule_text: Choose execution method (limit vs market order) based on trading frequency and cost structure: high-volume short-term traders should use limit orders to capture liquidity rebates and offset transaction costs, while accepting adverse selection risk.

Expected rebate income / expected transaction costs >= 0.60
Bid-ask spread < 2 ticks
Average trade hold time < 5 minutes
exit_conditions: Limit order not filled within 30 seconds
Switch to market order execution

exit_conditions: All order fragments completed
Time period elapsed with remaining size cancelled

rule_text: A trader should partition trading capital into a conservative base allocation and an aggressive allocation, using standard position-sizing rules on the base and aggressive sizing only on the segregated portion to limit downside risk from future losses exceeding historical assumptions.

Each trade is assigned to either base or aggressive allocation based on pre-defined criteria

parameters.aggressive_position_size_rule: Kelly or optimized sizing on aggressive portion only

exit_conditions: Exit all trades in both allocations at predefined loss limit or profit target
Rebalance capital partitions monthly or after significant drawdown

rule_text: Set risk per trade as a consistent percentage of account equity to limit downside exposure and ensure no single loss or streak of losses can eliminate the trading account.
entry_conditions: Trade setup identified with defined stop loss
Position size calculated such that max loss = 0.5% of current account equity
exit_conditions: Exit at stop loss (0.5% account loss)
Exit at profit target
Exit if consecutive losses reach 10 (5% total drawdown)

rule_text: A trader should account for the mathematical asymmetry between drawdown magnitude and recovery percentage required, adjusting position sizing and risk limits to prevent underestimation of true recovery burden.
entry_conditions: Current equity drawdown < 10% from peak
Projected maximum historical drawdown < 15%
Recovery ratio (recovery_return / initial_drawdown) < 1.3x
exit_conditions: Exit all positions if equity drops 10% from peak
Reduce position size by 50% for every 2% of drawdown

rule_text: A trader should only trade a system when the mathematical expectancy is positive, calculated as the probability-weighted sum of win/loss outcomes.

exit_conditions: Exit when system expectancy falls below 0.15R after recomputing with rolling window
Suspend trading and re-evaluate if win rate drops below historical baseline

exit_conditions: Actual P&L falls below 25th percentile of distribution over rolling window of 50 trades
Stop trading and re-evaluate system

Account can tolerate maximum 5% drawdown before reducing position size
Trader's investment goals prioritize steady compounding over explosive growth
exit_conditions: Exit when cumulative loss reaches 5% of starting capital
Reduce exposure if win rate declines below strategy baseline

rule_text: Size each position so that its daily P&L contribution is equalized across all holdings, using volatility (ATR) as the standard, resulting in larger positions in low-volatility instruments and smaller positions in high-volatility ones.
entry_conditions: Current ATR < 20-day average ATR (volatility not compressed)
Position size allocated based on target_daily_contribution / Current_ATR
exit_conditions: Reduce or close position if Current ATR < (20-day avg ATR × compression_threshold)
Monitor for volatility regime shift

rule_text: A trader should size positions by weighting both the probability of loss AND the expected magnitude of that loss, rejecting trades where either component is misjudged or overlooked.

rule_text: A trader should actively manage tail-risk exposure in low-probability, high-consequence scenarios rather than either ignoring them or obsessing over them, by allocating appropriate capital and protective measures proportional to the severity of potential loss.
entry_conditions: Portfolio concentration in single asset or strategy exceeds 15% of total capital
Implied volatility or realized volatility in portfolio constituents is elevated (>75th percentile)
Options market pricing indicates tail-risk premium (skew >0.15 standard deviations below ATM)

parameters.hedging_instrument: OTM puts 2+ standard deviations below current price

exit_conditions: Tail-risk premium decays to <0.05 SD
Position reduced to <10% of portfolio
Hedge expires profitably or time decay reaches 80% expiration

rule_text: A trader should actively manage tail-risk exposure in low-probability, high-consequence scenarios rather than either ignoring them or obsessing over them, by allocating appropriate capital and protective measures proportional to the severity of potential loss.

rule_text: A trader must adopt the belief that individual trade outcomes are uncertain, but profit over a sufficiently large set of trades is near-certain when trading with positive expectancy. This mindset separation—between single-trade risk and portfolio-level certainty—is the core discipline distinguishing profitable discretionary traders from others.
entry_conditions: Historical backtest shows ≥200 trades with positive expectancy
Win rate and risk/reward ratio are documented and stable
Trader has passed psychology checkpoint: can articulate edge without emotional defense
Drawdown tolerance is pre-defined based on portfolio size
exit_conditions: Exit trade per rule; review belief alignment after every 10 trades
If expectancy deteriorates, halt trading and revalidate system

rule_text: A trader must maintain professional-level performance records tracking every aspect of trading activity over a significant period to identify whether they possess a genuine edge; without this discipline, they cannot distinguish between luck and skill, and will consistently lose money.
entry_conditions: Minimum 500 completed trades in backtested/paper trading with full record
Win rate and average winner/loser ratio statistically significant at 95% confidence

Only then enter live trading with position sizing tied to verified edge
exit_conditions: If live performance diverges >20% from backtested edge metrics over 100 trades, halt and re-record
If monthly record shows no documented edge present, reduce position size by 50%

exit_conditions: Exit at predetermined stop or target
No discretionary hold extension

Exit immediately upon news of regulatory investigation or exchange issues

rule_text: A swing trader should enter positions only at precise pattern-identified points that indicate market imbalance, with predefined risk levels (stops), and manage toward capturing a single clean swing move rather than adding into adverse moves.

exit_conditions: Exit at predefined stop level (2.0 × ATR from entry)
Exit when pattern target reached (1.5 × measured swing distance)
Exit on close of day if swing target not achieved by end of session

Position sizing: 0.75% of net liquidation value risked per trade
exit_conditions: Exit at profit target: 1.0x risk distance from entry (1:1 risk-reward)
Stop loss at initial risk threshold (0.75% netliq)

rule_text: In high-volatility regimes with unpredictable price action, traders should either accept large stops with minimal position size OR restrict entries to high-confidence patterns; gap breakouts beyond pattern boundaries, while offering powerful directional shifts, require careful execution planning due to adverse fill risk.
entry_conditions: High-confidence pattern identified (e.g., high-and-tight flag)
Price breaks above previous day high
Entry occurs within pattern boundaries (no gap above pattern high)
Position size capped at 1-2% of account risk per trade
exit_conditions: Stop loss at 2.5x ATR below entry
Take profit at 2x risk-reward ratio

rule_text: A trader should trade complex pullback patterns on higher timeframes than the one where they appear, because complex pullbacks on lower timeframes typically represent simpler pullbacks on higher timeframes, resulting in stronger subsequent moves.
entry_conditions: Identify complex pullback on current timeframe (3+ touches/reversals)
Shift analysis to 2 timeframes higher
Confirm simple pullback structure exists on higher timeframe
Enter when pullback completes and price breaks prior extreme on higher timeframe
exit_conditions: Exit on break of pullback low (if long) on the higher timeframe
Scale out at 1x risk:reward minimum

Maximum holding period: single trading session if no continuation

Entry triggered within first 30% of breakout bar range above swing high

exit_conditions: Close above entry + 2 ATR

Time stop at 20 bars if no follow-through

rule_text: When market conditions show signs of overextension and complacency, execute disciplined technical patterns to capture windfall profits from the impending correction, rather than blindly fighting the trend without structure.
entry_conditions: Market shows overextension on 2+ timeframes (e.g., RSI > 75 on daily AND 4H)
Complacency indicator active (VIX < 12 OR put/call ratio < 0.6)
Disciplined technical pattern formed (e.g., verified double-top or trend failure)
Intuitive research bias supports reversal thesis
exit_conditions: Profit target hit (1.0x ATR from entry)
Stop loss at pattern invalidation point + 1 ATR
Time exit after 5-10 bars if target not hit

rule_text: A trader should initiate a counter-trend trade when price fails to break beyond a trend extreme after an extended consolidation period that is significantly longer than the preceding normal consolidations.
