Market then consolidates at trend extreme for 12-16 bars (4x normal length)
Price attempts to break trend extreme but closes back inside consolidation
Next bar closes further away from the trend extreme (confirmation)
exit_conditions: Exit on break back through the consolidation zone high/low
Exit on close back at trend extreme
Time-based stop after 20 bars if no directional progress

rule_text: Enter countertrend trades at failure tests of climactic structure on lower timeframes when higher timeframe structure is in continuation, using disciplined structure-aligned entries rather than aggressive early counters.
entry_conditions: Higher timeframe (weekly+) displays bullish continuation pattern setup
Lower timeframe (daily or hourly) creates climactic structure with exhaustion

Failure test occurs: re-test does not break the extreme, reverses
Entry on close of failure test candle with 3-timeframe alignment confirmed
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks the extreme that was re-tested
Exit if higher timeframe pattern invalidates
Trail stop to first structure support

rule_text: A trader should avoid initiating entries during parabolic climaxes, and instead use parabolic price action as a setup confirmation for countertrendtrading entries executed at better technical levels, maintaining rigid discipline to resist emotional crowd reactions.
entry_conditions: Parabolic acceleration identified (price rising >2 std devs above 20-period MA)
Price retraces 50-61.8% of parabolic leg
Secondary technical support confirmed (prior swing low or MA confluence)
Rigid discipline: do not enter until ALL conditions met
exit_conditions: Exit at breakeven or -1R if parabolic resumes upward
Exit at +2R if support holds and reversal confirmed

rule_text: A trader should enter counter-trend positions on a retest of the previous trend extreme after a failed breakout, rather than chasing the initial breakout, to exploit the panic liquidation that occurs when trapped long traders exit.
entry_conditions: Price breaks to new high (or low in downtrend) in parabolic move
Price consolidates and pulls back
Price retests the previous swing extreme (within 0.5% of extreme)
Rejection candle forms (close back below/above extreme with volume spike)
RSI divergence confirms rejection (lower high in RSI despite price retesting)
exit_conditions: Exit if price breaks above/below retest level by 1% (stop loss)
Exit at 50% of the distance from retest to the prior consolidation low/high (profit target)

rule_text: A trader should enter counter-trend positions on a retest of the previous trend extreme after a failed breakout, rather than chasing the initial breakout, to exploit the panic liquidation that occurs when trapped long traders exit.
entry_conditions: Price breaks to new high (or low in downtrend)
Price retraces back to test the breakout level (within 1% of extreme)
Price closes back inside the prior trend channel or consolidation zone
Volume on the retest is at least 1.2x the 20-period average
exit_conditions: Stop loss placed 2.0 ATR above/below the retest level
Profit target at 100% of the initial failed move distance (retest to breakout level)

rule_text: A trader should place protective stops just below recent support levels (prior day lows) when holding positions in strong trends, adjusting stops tighter as price extends, to balance holding core positions against catastrophic loss while allowing trends to continue.
entry_conditions: Price in confirmed strong uptrend (higher highs, higher lows)
Recent consolidation or pullback (high-and-tight pattern)
Volume confirms accumulation during consolidation
exit_conditions: Price closes below stop level (1.5% below prior day low)
Trend structure breaks (lower high formed)
Volume surge with reversal price action

exit_conditions: Stop loss at 110% of climax high
Exit on close above consolidation high (trend resumption signal)

rule_text: A trader should reject weak bounces in downtrends as continuation signals and instead use sharp breaks of prior structure as entries, placing stops at discrete levels that offer asymmetric risk/reward rather than arbitrary intermediate points.
entry_conditions: Price breaks below the prior bar's low in a downtrend
Preceding bounce is visibly slower/weaker than prior bullish moves (reluctant price action)
Bounce failed to reclaim prior resistance or reach prior highs
Volume on bounce is lighter than prior rally attempts
exit_conditions: Stop hit at bounce extreme + 1.5 ATR
Close above the high of the setup bounce

rule_text: A trader should initiate a reversal trade when price tests a volatility band extreme multiple times followed by a failed breakout attempt, signaling trend exhaustion and overextension.
entry_conditions: Price tests volatility band (Bollinger Band or equivalent) at least 3 times within 5-8 bars
Last test of band is followed by a reversal bar that closes opposite to the direction of the test
Reversal bar's range is at least 50% of the preceding move range
Volume on reversal bar is above 20-period average
exit_conditions: Exit at opposite volatility band or 2x ATR from entry
Hard stop 1.5x ATR below entry (for long reversal)

Volume condition: not required but preferred above average
exit_conditions: Exit at opposite volatility band or 1.5x ATR from entry
Hard stop 1.2x ATR below entry (for long reversal)

rule_text: An edge in technical patterns comes from imbalance in buying and selling pressure; if price stalls near entry, the market shows balance and the pattern has no edge.
entry_conditions: Technical pattern identified (Anti, or similar)
Price moves at least 1.5% away from entry within 2 bars
No reversal back toward entry within 3 bars of entry
exit_conditions: Price trades back within 0.5% of entry price over 4+ bars (signals balance/no edge)
Stop loss at 2% from entry

rule_text: A trader should enter trades at support and resistance levels based on whether price is breaking through or failing to break through these levels, with breakouts representing continuation plays and failed breakouts representing reversal/hold plays.
entry_conditions: Price breaks beyond support or resistance level by minimum 2 ATR
Pullback occurs back toward level (within 50% of breakout move)
On lower timeframe (1-2 bars below primary), failure test pattern forms confirming hold
Volume confirms the reversal at support/resistance
exit_conditions: Price closes back through support/resistance level
Stop loss at swing low/high beyond entry point + 1 ATR

rule_text: Enter trades before the actual breakout by using early pattern formations (failure tests, lower timeframe pullbacks, small springs within the range) rather than waiting for the breakout itself, and adjust stops to respect the boundaries of the higher timeframe pattern.
entry_conditions: Price approaches resistance/support on higher timeframe
Price pulls back into the consolidation range (lower timeframe)
Pullback does not break previous support/resistance by more than 1% of range width
Volume confirms rejection at range boundary
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit at 2% beyond higher timeframe pattern extreme
Price breaks resistance decisively (>2% above previous high)

rule_text: Simple technical patterns become more powerful and tradeable when contextualized within larger market structure; a trader should enter reversion trades (pullbacks) in the direction of the dominant trend established by that broader structure.
entry_conditions: Price breaks below a consolidation/resistance on a higher timeframe (e.g., daily breaks from 4H consolidation)
Pullback occurs into the broken level within 2-3 bars of the breakdown
Enter when pullback retraces 50% of the initial breakdown leg
Volume or momentum indicator confirms pullback weakness
exit_conditions: Exit at swing high from prior trend leg
Exit if price closes above entry candle high and breaks structure

rule_text: Simple technical patterns become more powerful and tradeable when contextualized within larger market structure; a trader should enter reversion trades (pullbacks) in the direction of the dominant trend established by that broader structure.
entry_conditions: Price breaks below consolidation on current or 1 higher timeframe
Any pullback retracement within 61.8% of the breakdown leg
Enter on first touch of breakdown level or moving average (e.g., 20-period)
No requirement to wait for additional confirmation candles
exit_conditions: Exit at prior swing high or next technical resistance
Tight trailing stop based on intraday volatility

rule_text: A trader should prioritize high-probability setups with clean price action and avoid marginal trades, because capital is better deployed in highest-conviction opportunities rather than exhausted consolidation patterns.
entry_conditions: Price breaks a key level (support/resistance)
Breakout occurs on first or second attempt only
Price action shows clean continuation in direction of breakout within 1-2 bars
exit_conditions: First failure test of the breakout level
Close below/above breakout level (depending on direction) on any bar after entry
More than 20 bars of sloppy consolidation post-entry

rule_text: A trader should prioritize high-probability setups with clean price action and avoid marginal trades, because capital is better deployed in highest-conviction opportunities rather than exhausted consolidation patterns.
entry_conditions: Price tests a key level exactly 3 times
Third attempt shows failure test pattern (rejection candle)
Probability shift confirmed by price action reversal
exit_conditions: Second failure test of entry level after breakout
More than 30 bars of consolidation without directional progress
Price violates original breakout level by 2x the pullback range

rule_text: A trader should prioritize high-probability setups with clean price action and avoid marginal trades, because capital is better deployed in highest-conviction opportunities rather than exhausted consolidation patterns.
entry_conditions: Price makes 4 or more attempts at a level
Pattern of failures becomes visually obvious and repetitive
Consolidation period extends 40+ bars, indicating trapped traders
exit_conditions: Third or greater failure test after aggressive entry
Consolidation exceeds 50 bars post-entry
Price moves 3x the average pullback range against position

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch.10 (P1:OTA), pp.307-360

Breakout Failures
Therearethreecommonbreakoutfailures.Noneofthemneedextensiveanalysisatthis
point,becausetheyarepatternfailureswehavealreadyconsideredelsewhere.However,
fortradersfocusingonbreakouts,i
rule_text: A trader should prepare to manage risk and exit when a breakout fails at the breakout level itself, as evidenced by a failure test (price breaks past a level but then reverses back through it).
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level with volume confirmation
Entry taken at breakout point or on pullback to broken resistance
exit_conditions: Close back below (or above if short) the original breakout level
Exit on close of the bar that violates the level

rule_text: Enter long positions on the first pullback following a breakout, as this entry point often coincides with the inception of a new trend, while remaining cautious of failed breakouts that can reverse violently.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above prior resistance/consolidation high
Price pulls back to within 0.5-1.0 ATR of the breakout level
Entry only after a close above the breakout level following pullback
Volume on entry bar > 50th percentile of recent 20-bar average
exit_conditions: Close below the first consolidation low
Time-based exit if no new high within 10 bars

rule_text: A trader should distinguish between simple and complex post-breakout pullbacks, as simple pullbacks signal trend urgency while complex pullbacks indicate overextension; manage trades accordingly based on pullback structure complexity.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above resistance level
Pullback structure is simple (single leg, minimal retracement)
Pullback retraces less than 38.2% of initial breakout move
Pullback reversal occurs within 2 bars of pullback start
exit_conditions: Close below pullback low
Price fails to make higher high within 5 bars of pullback end

source_citation: The Art and Science of Technical Analysis, Adam Grimes, Ch.10 (OTA), pp.307-360
raw_chunk_text:
In the best breakout examples, a strong trend will quickly follow and this strong trend
will not generate complex pullbacks early on. Traders can trade these postbreakout
pullbacks with fairly tight s
rule_text: A trader should enter breakout trades early (in the base) when anticipating a breakout, but must accept the risk that the breakout may fail; alternatively, wait for confirmation of the breakout and trade pullbacks with tight stops relative to the pullback geometry, accepting later entry but higher probability.
entry_conditions: Price breaks above established resistance level with volume confirmation
Price pulls back after breakout
Pullback does not breach 50% of initial breakout move
exit_conditions: Stop hit at 0.5x ATR of pullback range
Close below pullback low
Time-based exit after 10 bars if no continuation

entry_conditions: Price consolidating in defined base (3-5 bars with decreasing range)
Base is near identified resistance level
Prior trend context supports upside bias
exit_conditions: Stop hit at 1.0x ATR below entry
Breakout fails and closes below base low
Time-based exit after 15 bars if range-bound

rule_text: A trader must explicitly choose between early entry with superior trade location but lower probability, or late entry with inferior location but higher probability, and then construct a clear plan that emphasizes loss management as much as profit taking.
entry_conditions: Price closes beyond pattern boundary (e.g., breakout above resistance)
Volume or volatility confirmation present at breakout point
Pattern identified in advance with clear invalidation level
exit_conditions: Stop loss triggered at pattern invalidation level (e.g., reclose below breakout)
Profit target at 1.0x to 1.5x initial risk
Trailing stop after breakout confirmation with 0.5% buffer

rule_text: A trader should align their stop placement and position management with market structure and natural price behavior rather than fight against it; repeated emotional frustration from stops being hit indicates misalignment with market nature and requires behavioral modification.
entry_conditions: Identify established trade setup based on technical pattern
Calculate ATR over last 20 periods
Place initial stop 2.5x ATR away from entry point
exit_conditions: Hit initial stop loss at 2.5x ATR
Trailing stop at 1.5x ATR once profitable
Close position if setup structure breaks

rule_text: A trader must discipline and control their natural aggression rather than suppress or unleash it, calibrating intensity to market conditions and emotional state to preserve capital and decision quality.
entry_conditions: Emotional intensity self-assessment >= 7/10 (elevated aggression detected)
Recent consecutive wins or losses triggering hormonal response
Urge to increase position size or revenge trade is present
exit_conditions: Exit all positions and stand aside for remainder of trading session
Emotional intensity drops below 4/10

rule_text: A trader must cease trading activity immediately upon detecting signs of emotional loss of control (tilt), as the brain in this state cannot produce sound trading decisions.
entry_conditions: Trader detects any increase in anger, frustration, or sense of loss of control toward the market
Trading decisions are being made with speed/aggression inconsistent with personal baseline
Self-awareness check: trader cannot articulate the rationale for last 2 trades
exit_conditions: Resume trading only after 60 minutes of non-trading reflection
Trader has documented the emotional trigger and written a single plan before re-entering

rule_text: When experiencing tilt (emotional/chemical compromise), a trader must immediately exit losing positions and place protective stops on remaining open trades to remove themselves from the decision-making process until emotional equilibrium is restored.
entry_conditions: Trader self-identifies emotional compromise (tilt)
ANY open position exists
exit_conditions: All open positions closed at market
No new trades entered until next trading session

rule_text: A trader experiencing emotional disturbance should systematically restore psychological control through graded re-entry before resuming normal position sizing.
entry_conditions: Emotional disturbance or loss event occurs
Trader completes written journaling and deep breathing
Meditation session completed (minimum 10 minutes)
Minimum 2-hour break elapsed since triggering event
exit_conditions: Exit test trade at first profit or loss (close after 1-3 bars)
If psychological reaction to test trade is negative, trigger extended break

exit_conditions: Do not modify strategy based on analysis of outliers alone
Require pattern to appear in median/mode outcome, not just extremes

rule_text: A trader should regularly test their ability to deliberately trade against their own methodology to expose overconfidence bias and verify that results come from skill rather than randomness.
entry_conditions: Trader has completed 90+ days of forward trading with methodology
Win rate or profit factor has exceeded historical baseline by >15%
Trader experiences emotional confidence surge or overconfidence symptoms (excessive position sizing, reduced stop discipline)
exit_conditions: Complete 20 reverse-methodology trades
Analyze if reverse-trade P&L is statistically significantly worse than forward trades (p < 0.05)
If reverse trades perform similarly to forward trades, suspend methodology for review

rule_text: A trader must actively separate emotional attachment from trading decisions and exit losing positions immediately and unemotionally, treating being wrong as inevitable rather than painful.
entry_conditions: Trade initiated with predetermined loss threshold
Initial invalidation signal triggered (e.g., break of entry support)
exit_conditions: Exit immediately at first technical invalidation (support break, trend reversal signal)
Exit at emotional checkpoint if trader acknowledges emotional attachment
Exit after 2 bars if still in loss after invalidation signal

rule_text: Assume every trade is wrong at entry; actively seek disconfirming evidence rather than confirmation. This mindset eliminates confirmation bias, reduces emotional pressure, and enables rational position management.
entry_conditions: Trade initiated based on technical or fundamental thesis
Immediately assume the thesis is incorrect
Require evidence contradicting the initial thesis within first 2% of risk capital loss
exit_conditions: Exit if no disconfirming evidence appears after 2% loss
Exit if thesis is disproven before position gains
Hold only if surprised by immediate market behavior validating position

Position sizing constrained to micro-lots or paper trading only until threshold met

Return to simulation/reduced sizing for emotional recalibration

rule_text: A trader using a hybrid system (discretionary interventions on top of systematic rules) must rigorously validate that interventions add value by comparing hybrid results to raw system results, and must ensure the underlying system itself demonstrates profitability in both backtests and forward tests before deploying it.
entry_conditions: Raw system shows Sharpe ratio ≥ 1.0 in backtest
Raw system shows Sharpe ratio ≥ 0.8 in forward test (minimum 3 months)
Hybrid model has outperformed raw system in ≥ 80% of rolling 20-trade windows
No intervention allowed if made within 5 bars of system signal (prevents emotional reaction)
exit_conditions: Hybrid Sharpe ratio falls below raw system Sharpe ratio for 2 consecutive months
Suspend all interventions and revert to raw system

exit_conditions: Exit if drawdown exceeds 25% of starting capital despite accepting losses
Reduce position size only if account equity falls below 75% of initial

rule_text: A trader must establish and document a comprehensive trading plan that includes position sizing, capital risk limits, trade management rules, and decision frequency, then execute that plan with strict adherence to maximize performance evaluation validity.

Weekly loss limit breached: cease trading remainder of week
Monthly loss limit breached: cease trading until next month

rule_text: A trader should follow their trading plan with precision and commit to the process of execution rather than pursue intellectual knowledge alone, because the gap between knowledge and execution is where most traders fail.

Risk parameters within ±2% of planned levels
exit_conditions: Exit rule from plan is triggered
Stop loss at planned level is hit
Any deviation >2% triggers plan review

rule_text: A trader should maintain a detailed written trading journal to detect performance deterioration caused by external stress or psychological issues, enabling diagnosis of performance changes through systematic record review.
entry_conditions: Weekly review of all trades completed
Performance metric drops by 5% or more from 4-week rolling average
Trader documents psychological/emotional state before each trade
exit_conditions: Root cause identified and documented
Corrective action plan written and implemented
Performance returns to baseline within 2 weeks

rule_text: A trader should conduct statistical analysis of P&L segmented by market environment, asset class, and pattern type to identify hidden performance strengths and weaknesses, then track performance over time to detect early warning signs of degradation or style drift.
entry_conditions: Conduct P&L analysis at end of each month

Identify segments with positive P&L contribution >2% of total account

parameters.performance_warning_trigger: Any segment shows negative P&L for 2 consecutive months

exit_conditions: Stop trading identified weak segments immediately when underperformance threshold breached
Rebalance focus entirely to positive-performing segments

rule_text: A trader should reject or significantly scrutinize a trading system when statistical testing reveals that observed performance cannot be reliably distinguished from random chance, indicated by high p-values and volatility that dwarfs mean returns.
entry_conditions: Calculate p-value from closed trade distribution
Calculate ratio: StdDev(trades) / Mean(trade)
Only trade if p-value < 0.05 AND StdDev ratio < 10x
exit_conditions: Stop trading system if p-value drifts above 0.05 on rolling window of last 50 trades
Suspend if StdDev ratio exceeds 10x on new equity curve drawdown

rule_text: A trader should isolate and eliminate trade types that fail to produce positive expected value, even if individual metrics (win rate or reward/risk ratio) appear favorable in isolation, because only trades with positive mean outcomes across a statistically significant sample should remain in the playbook.
entry_conditions: Trade class has minimum 30 completed trades
Mean trade value is negative
Standard error of mean < mean trade value absolute value (t-statistic > 1.0)
exit_conditions: Remove trade type from playbook permanently

exit_conditions: Do not trade system; requires fundamental redesign

rule_text: Analyze the ratio of buyer-motivated trades (hitting the offer) versus seller-motivated trades (hitting the bid) to assess conviction level and urgency behind price moves; straight-line price movement with sustained offer-hitting indicates stronger buyer conviction than oscillating back-and-forth motion.

exit_conditions: Ratio falls below 1.5
Price reverses with sellers hitting bid consistently

rule_text: A trader should enter a spread position when the historical relationship between two correlated assets deviates from its expected premium/discount, betting on mean reversion of the spread rather than directional movement of either asset.
entry_conditions: Current spread (Asset A - Asset B) deviates >2.0 std dev from 20-period mean
Rolling 60-period correlation between Assets A and B > 0.70
Spread has been in same direction for minimum 5 bars
exit_conditions: Spread reverts to within 0.5 std dev of mean
Correlation drops below 0.60
Maximum holding period: 20 bars

rule_text: Use EMA instead of SMA for faster reaction to price shocks and trend initiations, but recognize that SMA recovers more quickly when trends end—choose based on market regime.

Trade only when volatility regime is stable (ATR < median ATR of last 50 bars)
exit_conditions: Price crosses back through SMA with close on opposite side
Trend reversal signal confirmed by 2+ consecutive opposite candles

rule_text: A trader should prefer exponential moving averages (EMA) over simple moving averages (SMA) in systematic trading systems, particularly in high-volatility or shock-prone environments, because SMAs are distorted by outliers and price shocks that temporarily leave the evaluation window.
entry_conditions: Price crosses above/below EMA
EMA smoothness ratio (current bar variance) < 0.3
No earnings announcement or economic event within 48 hours
exit_conditions: Price closes opposite side of EMA by buffer amount
Volatility regime shift detected (20-period ATR increases >50%)

Price has NOT confirmed reversal with a lower low or close below support
exit_conditions: Price closes above prior swing high with volume confirmation
MACD histogram turns positive and price confirms with breakout

rule_text: A price move accompanied by weakening or diverging momentum indicator conviction is more likely to fail to continue, signaling potential reversal or exhaustion.
entry_conditions: Price makes a new 14-bar high (or low)
MACD histogram fails to make new high (or new low) — same-bar or previous bar comparison
Divergence visible in current bar
exit_conditions: Exit 50% at nearest support/resistance after divergence signal
Trail remaining position by 1 ATR
Stop loss: 2 ATR from entry

rule_text: A trader should initiate directional trades during range expansion phases when volatility measures and bar ranges are increasing, as these conditions indicate probable sustained directional movement.
entry_conditions: Current bar range > 1.5× average of prior 20 bar ranges
ATR(14) > SMA(ATR, 20) + 1 standard deviation
Price closes beyond prior 20-bar high or low
Entry only after 2 consecutive bars confirming expansion
exit_conditions: Exit on close below 10-period moving average
Exit if range contraction occurs for 3 consecutive bars

rule_text: A trader should build positions through scaled entries into adverse price movement, using firm risk management rules to prevent catastrophic losses from oversized positions.
entry_conditions: Price moves against intended direction by 1-2% from initial entry signal
Cumulative position size remains below 50% of max allocation
Each tranche is 15% of total intended position size
exit_conditions: Full position stop-loss triggered at -4% on aggregate position
Maximum 3 tranches entered; no further scaling if 4th trigger fires
Exit first tranche if adverse move exceeds 3% before second tranche fills

exit_conditions: Full position stop-loss triggered at -6% on aggregate position
Maximum 4 tranches entered; halt scaling if adverse move exceeds 5%

rule_text: A trader should build positions through scaled entries into adverse price movement, using firm risk management rules to prevent catastrophic losses from oversized positions.

Cumulative position size can reach 90% of max allocation
Each tranche is 33% of total intended position size

Maximum 5 tranches entered; continue scaling if signal remains valid
Profit-taking only after 5% gain on full position

rule_text: A trader should place stop-loss orders outside the noise level of the market at price levels where the trade thesis is decisively invalidated, balancing technical precision with risk management.
entry_conditions: Trade entry signal generated at support or resistance level
Identify recent price oscillations and ATR-based noise range
exit_conditions: Exit when price closes beyond stop-loss level
Stop placed 2.5 × ATR(20) away from entry price

rule_text: A trader should adjust position size based on account risk capital and risk per trade, using fixed fractional approaches to prevent catastrophic losses and preserve capital across multiple trades.
entry_conditions: Trade setup identified with defined risk
Account risk capital calculated
Maximum loss per trade ≤ 1% of account
exit_conditions: Stop loss hit (defined at entry)
Profit target reached
5 consecutive losses → reduce to 0.5% risk per trade
